---
format:
  dashboard:
    embed-resources: false
    scrolling: true
    theme:
      - default
      - lib/custom.scss
    include-in-header: "../www/cvm-spacing.html"
    nav-buttons: []
    orientation: rows
params:
  all_data_path: ""
  cohort_colors_json: ""
  period_index_json: ""
  render_ts: ""
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

# Anchor knitr to project root (Quarto otherwise runs chunks from templates/).
proj_root <- normalizePath("..", winslash = "/")
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = proj_root)

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(plotly); library(DT); library(htmltools)
  library(jsonlite)
})
for (f in c("utils.R", "data_layer.R", "portfolio_runner.R",
            "comparison_engine.R", "ai_interpret.R",
            "plot_helpers.R", "html_components.R")) {
  source(file.path(proj_root, "R", f), local = FALSE)
}

# ---- payload read ---------------------------------------------------------
all_data <- readRDS(params$all_data_path)
all_data <- Filter(function(r) !is.null(r) && length(r$periods) > 0, all_data)
stopifnot(length(all_data) >= 1)

# cohort_colors as a named character vector (source of truth, parsed from
# render_dashboard.R's computed assignment — do NOT recompute here).
cohort_colors <- jsonlite::fromJSON(params$cohort_colors_json, simplifyVector = TRUE)
if (!is.character(cohort_colors) || length(cohort_colors) == 0) {
  # Defensive fallback: compute from labels if caller forgot to pass.
  cohort_colors <- assign_cohort_colors(vapply(all_data, function(r) r$cohort_label, character(1)))
}

period_index_json <- params$period_index_json
cohort_colors_json <- params$cohort_colors_json

primary   <- all_data[[length(all_data)]]
render_ts <- if (nzchar(params$render_ts %||% "")) {
  params$render_ts
} else {
  format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
}

# Ensure all_data is keyed by cohort_label so per-cohort lookups stay clean.
names(all_data) <- vapply(all_data, function(r) r$cohort_label, character(1))

# ---- helpers scoped to this template --------------------------------------
# cohort_color_for: safe lookup that falls through to navy if missing.
cohort_color_for <- function(cohort_label) {
  col <- cohort_colors[[cohort_label]]
  if (is.null(col) || is.na(col) || !nzchar(col)) CVM_PALETTE$navy else col
}
# Raw-HTML fence helper — same pattern as other templates; required so
# pandoc does not re-parse inline <span> tags as markdown text.
emit_html <- function(tag) {
  if (is.null(tag)) return(invisible())
  cat("\n```{=html}\n", as.character(tag), "\n```\n\n", sep = "")
}

# ---- overview scalars ------------------------------------------------------
overall_medians <- vapply(all_data, function(r) r$kpi$overall_median %||% NA_real_, numeric(1))
total_students  <- sum(vapply(all_data, function(r) r$kpi$total_students  %||% 0L, integer(1)))
total_periods   <- sum(vapply(all_data, function(r) r$kpi$periods_covered %||% 0L, integer(1)))
n_tag_sets_max  <- max(vapply(all_data, function(r) r$kpi$n_tag_sets %||% 0L, integer(1)), 0L)

# Pooled IQR — quartiles of every cohort's capped grade values pooled
# together. Drives the hero KPI subvalue so the median + IQR tell the
# same distributional story.
pooled_grades <- unlist(lapply(all_data, function(r) {
  if (!isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok) || is.null(r$grades_df) || nrow(r$grades_df) == 0) {
    return(numeric(0))
  }
  g_col <- if ("grade_display" %in% names(r$grades_df)) {
    r$grades_df$grade_display
  } else r$grades_df$grade
  g_col[is.finite(g_col)]
}), use.names = FALSE)
pooled_q1 <- if (length(pooled_grades)) as.numeric(stats::quantile(pooled_grades, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)) else NA_real_
pooled_q3 <- if (length(pooled_grades)) as.numeric(stats::quantile(pooled_grades, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)) else NA_real_

# ---- per-cohort precomputed frames (used by Assessment Performance tab) ----
cohort_trends <- lapply(all_data, function(r) {
  if (!isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok)) return(NULL)
  median_trend(r$grades_df)
})

# cohort_thresholds: count of students with <70%, <80%, <90% final grade.
# Shape: tibble(threshold = <chr>, count = <int>)
thresholds_from_gb <- function(gb) {
  final <- gb |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$assessment %in% c("weighted_overall_grade",
                                          "individual_weighted_overall"))
  if (nrow(final) == 0) final <- gb
  g <- final$grade
  tibble::tibble(
    threshold = c("< 70%", "< 80%", "< 90%"),
    count     = c(sum(g < 70, na.rm = TRUE),
                  sum(g < 80, na.rm = TRUE),
                  sum(g < 90, na.rm = TRUE))
  )
}
cohort_thresholds <- lapply(all_data, function(r) {
  if (!isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok) || is.null(r$grades_df) || nrow(r$grades_df) == 0) return(NULL)
  thresholds_from_gb(r$grades_df)
})

# period_labels: "34" -> "GC26 · Fall Y1". Drives readable x-axis ticks on the
# trend chart. Built from build_period_index() which already does this work.
pi <- build_period_index(all_data)
period_labels <- stats::setNames(
  vapply(pi, function(p) as.character(p$label), character(1)),
  vapply(pi, function(p) as.character(p$cperiod_id), character(1))
)

# cohort_stats: per-cohort summary rows below the trend chart.
# Stats use the capped `grade_display` column (extra-credit weighted formulas
# can push raw grades above 100; capping at 100 avoids nonsense medians).
# The capping flag (`had_grade_capping` on each cohort) drives the asterisk
# + footnote in the Overview cohort summary table.
cohort_stats <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
  r <- all_data[[lab]]
  if (!isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok) || is.null(r$grades_df) || nrow(r$grades_df) == 0) {
    return(tibble::tibble(
      Cohort = lab, Median = NA_real_,
      Q1 = NA_real_, Q3 = NA_real_,
      Mean = NA_real_, SD = NA_real_,
      N = 0L, `% ≥ 90` = NA_real_, `% ≥ 80` = NA_real_, `% < 70` = NA_real_,
      Capped = FALSE,
      .rows = 1
    ))
  }
  # Use the final-grade assessments where available; fall back to all rows.
  final <- r$grades_df |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$assessment %in% c("weighted_overall_grade",
                                          "individual_weighted_overall"))
  df <- if (nrow(final) > 0) final else r$grades_df
  g_raw <- df$grade
  g_col <- if ("grade_display" %in% names(df)) df$grade_display else pmin(g_raw, 100)
  g <- g_col[!is.na(g_col)]
  tibble::tibble(
    Cohort   = lab,
    Median   = stats::median(g),
    Q1       = as.numeric(stats::quantile(g, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)),
    Q3       = as.numeric(stats::quantile(g, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)),
    Mean     = mean(g),
    SD       = stats::sd(g),
    N        = dplyr::n_distinct(df$id),
    `% ≥ 90` = 100 * mean(g >= 90),
    `% ≥ 80` = 100 * mean(g >= 80),
    `% < 70` = 100 * mean(g <  70),
    Capped   = isTRUE(r$had_grade_capping) ||
               any(g_raw > 100, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}))

# cohorts_with_data: keep the order of names(all_data) but drop any that have
# no gradebook — used by the histogram grid below so we don't render empty
# panels.
cohorts_with_data <- Filter(function(lab) {
  r <- all_data[[lab]]
  isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok) && !is.null(r$grades_df) && nrow(r$grades_df) > 0
}, names(all_data))

# cohort_breakdown_df: single long table for the breakdown DT — one row per
# assessment × cohort × period.
cohort_breakdown_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
    r <- all_data[[lab]]
    if (is.null(r$grades_df) || nrow(r$grades_df) == 0) return(NULL)
    bd <- assessment_breakdown(r$grades_df)
    if (nrow(bd) == 0) return(NULL)
    bd$cohort <- lab
    bd$cohort_color <- unname(cohort_colors[[lab]])
    bd
  })
)

# Tag-sets available are derived from the primary (latest) cohort's coverage.
tag_set_names <- if (!is.null(primary$tag_coverage) && length(primary$tag_coverage) > 0) {
  names(primary$tag_coverage[[1]] %||% list())
} else character(0)

# Chip list order for sidebar display (pretty names).
tag_set_pretty <- if (length(tag_set_names) > 0) pretty_tagset_name(tag_set_names) else character(0)

# ---- Download-button helpers ---------------------------------------------
# A render-stable slug used for every PNG/CSV/Excel filename so a folder of
# downloads from one render groups together cleanly.
ts_slug <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", render_ts)

# Common plotly::config() block — adds the toolbar's PNG-export camera and
# trims the lasso/select tools we don't use. Pass the resulting list with
# do.call(plotly::config, c(list(p), cvm_plotly_config_args())).
cvm_plotly_config_args <- function(filename_tag = "chart", height = 600, width = 1200) {
  list(
    displaylogo            = FALSE,
    responsive             = TRUE,
    toImageButtonOptions   = list(
      format   = "png",
      filename = sprintf("%s_%s_%s", primary$course_code, filename_tag, ts_slug),
      height   = height,
      width    = width,
      scale    = 2
    ),
    modeBarButtonsToAdd    = list("toImage"),
    modeBarButtonsToRemove = list("lasso2d", "select2d")
  )
}

# Common DT options block — turns on Buttons extension with CSV + Excel
# exports. The buttons land in the table's top toolbar (dom = "Bfrtip").
cvm_dt_buttons <- function(filename_tag = "table") {
  # Compact icon-style labels — "↓ CSV" / "↓ Excel" — kept right-aligned
  # via .dt-buttons CSS in the trailing style block (Brand Guardian: don't
  # let download chrome compete visually with the table data).
  list(
    list(extend = "csv",
         text   = "↓ CSV",
         filename = sprintf("%s_%s", primary$course_code, filename_tag)),
    list(extend = "excel",
         text   = "↓ Excel",
         filename = sprintf("%s_%s", primary$course_code, filename_tag))
  )
}

# Tag-coverage data, serialized as JSON for the heatmap CSV-download
# button. Built once at setup time and injected into the rendered HTML
# via the trailing raw-HTML block.
tag_data_json <- if (length(tag_set_names) > 0) {
  jsonlite::toJSON(
    stats::setNames(
      lapply(tag_set_names, function(ts) {
        stats::setNames(
          lapply(all_data, function(r) {
            if (is.null(r$tag_coverage) || length(r$tag_coverage) == 0) return(list())
            merged <- dplyr::bind_rows(Filter(Negate(is.null),
                                              lapply(r$tag_coverage, function(x) x[[ts]])))
            as.list(tagset_counts_vec(merged))
          }),
          names(all_data)
        )
      }),
      tag_set_names
    ),
    auto_unbox = TRUE
  )
} else "{}"
```

---
title: "`r sprintf('%s — %s (All years)', primary$course_code, primary$course_name)`"
---

# {.sidebar}

```{r}
ui_sidebar(
  course_code = primary$course_code,
  course_name = primary$course_name,
  metadata = c(
    sprintf("All-years view — %d cohort%s",
            length(all_data),
            if (length(all_data) == 1) "" else "s"),
    sprintf("%d periods total", total_periods),
    sprintf("Rendered %s", render_ts)
  ),
  extra_sections = list(
    list(title = "Classes included",
         li_class = "cvm-cohort-item",
         items = vapply(all_data, function(r) r$cohort_label, character(1))),
    list(title = "Tag sets available",
         items = lapply(seq_along(tag_set_names), function(i) {
           ts <- tag_set_names[[i]]
           pretty <- tag_set_pretty[[i]]
           anchor_id <- paste0("tagset-", tolower(gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "-", ts)))
           # Always navigate to the Curriculum Tag Coverage tab first
           # (textContent match) then scroll to the matching section after
           # 350 ms so Bootstrap has time to swap tab panels. The keyword
           # match is alphanumeric-only on both sides, so case / spacing /
           # punctuation in tag-set names is tolerated.
           onclick_js <- gsub(
             "{TAG_LABEL}", ts,
             "(function(){
  var navLinks = document.querySelectorAll('.quarto-dashboard .navbar-nav .nav-link, .navbar-nav .nav-link');
  for (var i = 0; i < navLinks.length; i++) {
    var txt = navLinks[i].textContent.trim().toLowerCase();
    if (txt.includes('curriculum') || txt.includes('tag coverage')) {
      navLinks[i].click();
      break;
    }
  }
  setTimeout(function(){
    var anchors = document.querySelectorAll('[id]');
    var target = null;
    var keyword = '{TAG_LABEL}'.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]/g, '');
    for (var j = 0; j < anchors.length; j++) {
      var id = anchors[j].id.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]/g, '');
      if (id.includes(keyword)) { target = anchors[j]; break; }
    }
    if (target) target.scrollIntoView({behavior:'smooth', block:'start'});
  }, 350);
})()",
             fixed = TRUE
           )
           tags$a(
             href = paste0("#", anchor_id),
             style = "color:#C9A84C; text-decoration:none; cursor:pointer;",
             onclick = onclick_js,
             pretty
           )
         }))
  )
)
```

# Overview

## Row

```{r}
#| title: "Portfolio at a glance"
# KPI cards carry data-kpi hooks so the filter JS can recompute on chip
# toggle. Initial values reflect ALL cohorts selected.
tagList(
  ui_kpi_row(
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Pooled median grade (%)",
      value    = if (any(!is.na(overall_medians))) sprintf("%.1f", median(overall_medians, na.rm = TRUE)) else "—",
      subvalue = if (is.finite(pooled_q1) && is.finite(pooled_q3)) {
                   sprintf("IQR: %.1f – %.1f", pooled_q1, pooled_q3)
                 } else "Across selected cohorts",
      kpi_key  = "median"
    ),
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Total students assessed",
      value    = fmt_num(total_students),
      subvalue = "Summed across selected cohorts",
      kpi_key  = "students"
    ),
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Curriculum periods covered",
      value    = fmt_num(total_periods),
      kpi_key  = "periods"
    ),
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Curriculum tag sets",
      value    = fmt_num(n_tag_sets_max),
      subvalue = "Distinct curriculum tag sets",
      kpi_key  = "tag_sets",
      size     = "sm"
    )
  )
)
```

## Row

```{r}
#| title: "Cohort summary"
# One row per cohort. Median is rendered as a string ("95.4") — never with
# an asterisk on the value or the cohort name. The single capping footnote
# at the bottom of the Overview tab carries the disclaimer instead.
# Numeric columns go through DT::formatRound for consistent 1-decimal display.
rows <- lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
  r <- all_data[[lab]]
  col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
  stat <- cohort_stats[cohort_stats$Cohort == lab, , drop = FALSE]
  take_num <- function(col_name) {
    if (nrow(stat) == 0) return(NA_real_)
    v <- stat[[col_name]]
    if (length(v) == 0 || !is.finite(v[[1]])) NA_real_ else round(as.numeric(v[[1]]), 1)
  }
  med_num <- take_num("Median")
  median_display <- if (is.na(med_num)) "—" else sprintf("%.1f", med_num)
  q1_num <- take_num("Q1"); q3_num <- take_num("Q3")
  iqr_display <- if (is.na(q1_num) || is.na(q3_num)) "—"
                 else sprintf("%.1f – %.1f", q1_num, q3_num)
  data.frame(
    Cohort         = lab,
    Color          = col,
    Median         = median_display,
    `IQR (Q1–Q3)`  = iqr_display,
    Mean           = take_num("Mean"),
    SD             = take_num("SD"),
    `% ≥ 90`       = take_num("% ≥ 90"),
    `% ≥ 80`       = take_num("% ≥ 80"),
    `% < 70`       = take_num("% < 70"),
    N              = as.integer(r$kpi$total_students  %||% 0L),
    Periods        = as.integer(r$kpi$periods_covered %||% 0L),
    `Tag Sets`     = as.integer(r$kpi$n_tag_sets       %||% 0L),
    check.names    = FALSE,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})
summary_df <- do.call(rbind, rows)
# Cohort cell = colored dot + label. The containing <tr> is NOT wrapped in
# .cvm-filterable (DT rebuilds rows dynamically, and hiding rows via the
# filter JS would break DT's rendering). The chip filter bar drives the OTHER
# per-cohort cards and charts; this summary table intentionally always shows
# every cohort so the cross-cohort comparison stays visible.
summary_df$Cohort <- mapply(function(lab, col) {
  sprintf('<span style="display:inline-block;width:10px;height:10px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;margin-right:8px;vertical-align:middle;"></span>%s',
          col, htmlEscape(lab))
}, summary_df$Cohort, summary_df$Color, USE.NAMES = FALSE)
summary_df$Color <- NULL

# Spec-ordered columns: Cohort, Median, IQR (Q1–Q3), Mean, SD,
# % ≥ 90, % ≥ 80, % < 70, N, Periods, Tag Sets. Min/Max removed —
# they were misleading under extra-credit-inflated weighted formulas
# even after capping (a single 100 made every cohort look identical at
# the top end). IQR is the spread metric we want to report.
summary_df <- summary_df[, c("Cohort", "Median", "IQR (Q1–Q3)",
                             "Mean", "SD",
                             "% ≥ 90", "% ≥ 80", "% < 70",
                             "N", "Periods", "Tag Sets"), drop = FALSE]

# Pooled-stats row: same final-grade subset each cohort used, concatenated
# across all cohorts. Appended as one extra row at the bottom of the table
# with a navy-on-white visual treatment via DT rowCallback.
pooled_final <- unlist(lapply(all_data, function(r) {
  if (!isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok) || is.null(r$grades_df) || nrow(r$grades_df) == 0) {
    return(numeric(0))
  }
  final <- r$grades_df |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$assessment %in% c("weighted_overall_grade",
                                          "individual_weighted_overall"))
  df <- if (nrow(final) > 0) final else r$grades_df
  g_col <- if ("grade_display" %in% names(df)) df$grade_display else pmin(df$grade, 100)
  g_col[!is.na(g_col) & is.finite(g_col)]
}), use.names = FALSE)

if (length(pooled_final) > 0) {
  pooled_median  <- sprintf("%.1f", stats::median(pooled_final, na.rm = TRUE))
  pooled_q1      <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(pooled_final, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE))
  pooled_q3      <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(pooled_final, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE))
  pooled_iqr     <- sprintf("%.1f – %.1f", pooled_q1, pooled_q3)
  pooled_mean    <- round(mean(pooled_final, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
  pooled_sd      <- round(stats::sd(pooled_final, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
  pooled_pct90   <- round(100 * mean(pooled_final >= 90, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
  pooled_pct80   <- round(100 * mean(pooled_final >= 80, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
  pooled_pct70   <- round(100 * mean(pooled_final <  70, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
} else {
  pooled_median <- "—"
  pooled_iqr    <- "—"
  pooled_mean   <- NA_real_
  pooled_sd     <- NA_real_
  pooled_pct90  <- NA_real_
  pooled_pct80  <- NA_real_
  pooled_pct70  <- NA_real_
}
pooled_n       <- as.integer(sum(summary_df$N, na.rm = TRUE))
pooled_periods <- as.integer(sum(summary_df$Periods, na.rm = TRUE))
pooled_tagsets <- as.integer(max(summary_df$`Tag Sets`, na.rm = TRUE))

# Plain-text Cohort cell so the rowCallback below can match it by string;
# the navy/white/700 styling is supplied entirely by the rowCallback (no
# <strong> wrapper needed and none of the per-cohort dot prefix).
pooled_row <- data.frame(
  Cohort         = "All Cohorts (Pooled)",
  Median         = pooled_median,
  `IQR (Q1–Q3)`  = pooled_iqr,
  Mean           = pooled_mean,
  SD             = pooled_sd,
  `% ≥ 90`       = pooled_pct90,
  `% ≥ 80`       = pooled_pct80,
  `% < 70`       = pooled_pct70,
  N              = pooled_n,
  Periods        = pooled_periods,
  `Tag Sets`     = pooled_tagsets,
  check.names    = FALSE,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
summary_df <- rbind(summary_df, pooled_row)

# Always show all rows — cohorts + pooled row — no pagination ever.
# Future-proofs the table as new graduating classes are added: even if
# Class of 2029/2030/... appear, every row renders inline and the
# capping footnote always sits cleanly below the last row.
n_table_rows <- nrow(summary_df)  # includes the pooled row

# Median and IQR (Q1–Q3) are character columns; everything else stays
# numeric and goes through DT::formatRound for 1-decimal display.
numeric_cols <- c("Mean", "SD", "% ≥ 90", "% ≥ 80", "% < 70",
                  "N", "Periods", "Tag Sets")
right_align_idx0 <- which(names(summary_df) %in% c("Median", "IQR (Q1–Q3)",
                                                   numeric_cols)) - 1L

dt_widget <- DT::datatable(
  summary_df,
  rownames = FALSE,
  escape   = FALSE,
  extensions = "Buttons",
  options  = list(
    dom            = "Bfrtip",
    pageLength     = n_table_rows,
    lengthMenu     = list(c(n_table_rows, -1),
                          c(as.character(n_table_rows), "All")),
    paging         = FALSE,
    scrollCollapse = FALSE,
    ordering       = FALSE,
    scrollX        = FALSE,
    autoWidth      = TRUE,
    buttons        = cvm_dt_buttons("cohort_summary"),
    columnDefs = list(
      list(targets = 0, className = "dt-left", width = "140px"),
      list(targets = right_align_idx0, className = "dt-right")
    ),
    rowCallback = DT::JS(
      "function(row, data, index) {",
      "  if (data[0] === 'All Cohorts (Pooled)') {",
      "    $(row).css({'background-color':'#003F6B','color':'#FFFFFF','font-weight':'700'});",
      "    $('td', row).css({'background-color':'#003F6B','color':'#FFFFFF','border-top':'2px solid #AB8B00'});",
      "  }",
      "}"
    ),
    initComplete = DT::JS(
      "function(settings, json) {",
      "  $(this.api().table().header()).find('th').css({",
      "    'background-color': '#003F6B',",
      "    'color':            '#FFFFFF',",
      "    'font-weight':      '600',",
      "    'border-bottom':    '3px solid #AB8B00'",
      "  });",
      "}"
    )
  )
) |>
  DT::formatRound(columns = c("Mean", "SD",
                              "% ≥ 90", "% ≥ 80", "% < 70"), digits = 1)

dt_widget
```

# Assessment Performance

## Row

```{r}
# Assessment Performance — redesigned as five vertically stacked full-width
# sections. Each section has a navy 18px bold heading, a one-line muted
# description, 32px vertical padding, and is separated from the next by a
# 1px #E8ECF0 hr divider. The earlier chunk-isolation-per-widget
# requirement was only needed because of the `styleEqual(NULL, NULL)` /
# `onRender` bugs (both since removed), so all five widgets now live in a
# single tagList for a cleaner presentation flow.

section_head <- function(title, desc) {
  tagList(
    tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", title),
    tags$p(class  = "cvm-card-desc",  desc)
  )
}

# ── Section 0: Box plot of grade distribution by cohort ─────────────────
# Renders FIRST in Assessment Performance — gives the complete distributional
# picture (median, quartiles, outliers) for every cohort at a glance. The
# histograms in Section 2 follow below for granular shape detail.
s0_widget <- {
  any_grades <- any(vapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
    r <- all_data[[lab]]
    isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok) && !is.null(r$grades_df) && nrow(r$grades_df) > 0
  }, logical(1)))
  if (!any_grades) {
    ui_banner("No gradebook data available for any cohort.", variant = "info")
  } else {
    box_plot <- plotly::plot_ly(height = 360)
    for (lab in names(all_data)) {
      r <- all_data[[lab]]
      if (!isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok) || is.null(r$grades_df) ||
          nrow(r$grades_df) == 0) next
      final <- r$grades_df |>
        dplyr::filter(.data$assessment %in% c("weighted_overall_grade",
                                              "individual_weighted_overall"))
      df <- if (nrow(final) > 0) final else r$grades_df
      g_col <- if ("grade_display" %in% names(df)) df$grade_display
               else pmin(df$grade, 100)
      g <- g_col[is.finite(g_col)]
      if (length(g) == 0) next
      col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
      box_plot <- box_plot |>
        plotly::add_trace(
          type      = "box",
          y         = g,
          name      = lab,
          marker    = list(color = col),
          line      = list(color = col),
          fillcolor = paste0(col, "33"),
          boxpoints = "outliers",
          hovertemplate = paste0(lab, "<br>Median: %{median}<br>",
                                 "Q1: %{q1}<br>Q3: %{q3}<extra></extra>")
        )
    }
    box_plot <- box_plot |>
      plotly::layout(
        title = list(text = "Grade distribution by cohort — box plot",
                     font = list(color = CVM_PALETTE$navy, size = 14)),
        yaxis = list(title = "Grade (%)", range = c(0, 105),
                     gridcolor = "#E4E7EC"),
        xaxis = list(title = ""),
        paper_bgcolor = "white", plot_bgcolor = "white",
        font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", color = CVM_PALETTE$text),
        margin = list(t = 50, b = 60, l = 60, r = 40),
        showlegend = TRUE,
        legend = list(orientation = "h", y = -0.2)
      )
    box_plot <- do.call(plotly::config,
                        c(list(box_plot),
                          cvm_plotly_config_args(filename_tag = "grade_box_plot",
                                                 height = 600, width = 1200)))
    ui_plot_box(box_plot, height = 380)
  }
}

# ── Section 1: Grade Performance by Cohort ──────────────────────────────
s1_widget <- if (nrow(cohort_stats) == 0) {
  ui_banner("No gradebook data available for any cohort.", variant = "info")
} else {
  # Capped is an internal helper column for the Overview footnote — never
  # surface it in the Assessment Performance grid.
  stats_df <- cohort_stats[, setdiff(names(cohort_stats), "Capped"), drop = FALSE]
  stats_df$Cohort <- vapply(stats_df$Cohort, function(lab) {
    col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
    sprintf('<span style="display:inline-block;width:10px;height:10px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;margin-right:8px;vertical-align:middle;"></span>%s',
            col, htmlEscape(lab))
  }, character(1))

  numeric_cols_0idx <- which(!names(stats_df) %in% "Cohort") - 1L
  DT::datatable(
    stats_df, rownames = FALSE, escape = FALSE,
    extensions = "Buttons",
    options = list(
      dom = "Bfrtip", pageLength = 20, scrollX = FALSE, ordering = FALSE,
      buttons = cvm_dt_buttons("grade_performance"),
      columnDefs = list(
        list(targets = numeric_cols_0idx, className = "dt-right"),
        list(targets = 0, width = "200px")
      ),
      rowCallback = DT::JS(
        "function(row, data, dispIndex) {",
        "  if (dispIndex % 2 === 1) { $(row).css('background-color', '#F9FAFB'); }",
        "}"
      ),
      initComplete = DT::JS(
        "function(settings, json) {",
        "  $(this.api().table().header()).find('th').css({",
        "    'background-color': '#003F6B',",
        "    'color':            '#FFFFFF',",
        "    'font-weight':      '600'",
        "  });",
        "}"
      )
    )
  ) |>
    DT::formatRound(columns = c("Median", "Q1", "Q3", "Mean", "SD",
                                "% ≥ 90", "% ≥ 80", "% < 70"), digits = 1)
}

# ── Section 2: Grade Distribution by Cohort ─────────────────────────────
s2_widget <- if (length(cohorts_with_data) == 0) {
  ui_banner("No gradebook data available for any cohort.", variant = "info")
} else {
  tags$div(
    # Fix 1: fixed 3-column grid at 360px rows — see .cvm-histogram-grid
    # rule in the <style> block. No auto-fit / no minmax — overlap-proof.
    class = "cvm-histogram-grid",
    lapply(cohorts_with_data, function(lab) {
      r <- all_data[[lab]]
      col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
      tags$div(
        class = "cvm-filterable cvm-histogram-card",
        `data-cohort-labels` = lab,
        style = "background:#fff;border:1px solid #E0E4EC;border-radius:12px;padding:16px;position:relative;min-height:500px;height:auto;overflow:visible;",
        tags$div(
          style = "display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;margin-bottom:8px;",
          tags$span(style = sprintf(
            "display:inline-block;width:12px;height:12px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;",
            col)),
          tags$span(style = sprintf(
            "font-size:13px;font-weight:600;color:%s;", col),
            lab)
        ),
        ui_plot_box(
          do.call(plotly::config, c(
            list(plotly::ggplotly(
              distribution_plot(r$grades_df,
                                title = sprintf("Distribution — %s", lab),
                                cohort_color = col),
              height = 460
            )),
            cvm_plotly_config_args(filename_tag = sprintf("distribution_%s",
                                                          gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", lab)))
          )),
          height = 460
        )
      )
    })
  )
}

# ── Section 3: Students Below Grade Thresholds ──────────────────────────
s3_widget <- ui_plot_box(
  thresholds_plotly_multi(cohort_thresholds, cohort_colors, height = 420),
  height = 440
)

# ── Section 4: Assessment Breakdown by Cohort ───────────────────────────
s4_widget <- if (nrow(cohort_breakdown_df) == 0) {
  ui_banner("No gradebook breakdown available for any cohort.", variant = "info")
} else {
  per_cohort <- cohort_breakdown_df |>
    dplyr::group_by(.data$assessment, .data$cohort) |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      median = stats::median(.data$median, na.rm = TRUE),
      n      = sum(.data$n, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  per_cohort$display <- ifelse(
    is.na(per_cohort$median) | !is.finite(per_cohort$median),
    NA_character_,
    sprintf("%.1f (%d)", round(per_cohort$median, 1), as.integer(per_cohort$n))
  )
  wide <- per_cohort |>
    dplyr::select("assessment", "cohort", "display") |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "cohort", values_from = "display")
  wide$Type <- assessment_type_from_name(wide$assessment)
  cohort_cols <- intersect(names(all_data), names(wide))
  wide <- wide |>
    dplyr::select("Type", "Assessment" = "assessment", all_of(cohort_cols)) |>
    dplyr::arrange(.data$Type, .data$Assessment)

  type_col_idx   <- which(names(wide) == "Type") - 1L
  assess_col_idx <- which(names(wide) == "Assessment") - 1L
  cohort_idx     <- which(names(wide) %in% cohort_cols) - 1L
  js_cohort_idx  <- paste0("[", paste(cohort_idx, collapse = ","), "]")
  rowcb <- DT::JS(paste0(
    "function(row, data, dispIndex) {",
    "  var idx = ", js_cohort_idx, ";",
    "  idx.forEach(function(i){",
    "    var c = $('td:eq('+(i)+')', row);",
    "    var t = (c.text() || '').trim();",
    "    if (t === '' || t === 'NA') {",
    "      c.text('Not offered').css({'color':'#9AA5B1','font-style':'italic','text-align':'center'});",
    "    } else {",
    "      c.css({'text-align':'right','font-variant-numeric':'tabular-nums'});",
    "    }",
    "  });",
    "  if (dispIndex % 2 === 1) { $(row).css('background-color', '#F9FAFB'); }",
    "  $('td:eq('+(", type_col_idx, ")+')', row).css({'color':'#5A6472','font-size':'11px','text-transform':'uppercase','letter-spacing':'0.05em','font-weight':'600'});",
    "}"
  ))

  DT::datatable(
    wide, rownames = FALSE, escape = FALSE,
    extensions = "Buttons",
    options = list(
      dom = "Bfrtip", buttons = cvm_dt_buttons("assessment_breakdown"),
      pageLength = 50, scrollX = FALSE, ordering = TRUE,
      columnDefs = list(
        list(targets = type_col_idx,   width = "130px", className = "dt-left"),
        list(targets = assess_col_idx, className = "dt-left"),
        list(targets = cohort_idx,     className = "dt-right", width = "120px")
      ),
      rowCallback = rowcb,
      initComplete = DT::JS(
        "function(settings, json) {",
        "  $(this.api().table().header()).find('th').css({",
        "    'background-color': '#003F6B',",
        "    'color':            '#FFFFFF',",
        "    'font-weight':      '600'",
        "  });",
        "}"
      )
    )
  )
}

# Section 5 removed — duplicated Overview cohort summary + Section 1.

# ── Compose: stack the five sections vertically ─────────────────────────
# Each section is a white card per spec: width:100%;display:block;clear:both;
# margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;background:#FFFFFF;border-radius:12px;
# box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07). Inline style keeps the spec
# self-evident at the call site; the .cvm-card class mirrors the same
# values and also wires scoped table styling (Fix 4).
card_style <- paste0(
  "width:100%;display:block;clear:both;",
  "margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;",
  "background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-radius:12px;",
  "box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);"
)

tagList(
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-card",
    style = card_style,
    section_head(
      "Grade Distribution Overview",
      "Box plot of final grades per cohort — median (center line), IQR (box), and outliers in one view. Histograms below show the shape in more detail."
    ),
    s0_widget
  ),
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-card",
    style = card_style,
    section_head(
      "Grade Performance by Cohort",
      "Overall grade statistics for each graduating class — median, spread, and the share meeting pass thresholds."
    ),
    s1_widget
  ),
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-card",
    style = card_style,
    section_head(
      "Grade Distribution by Cohort",
      "Histogram of final grades for each cohort — shape and spread side by side."
    ),
    s2_widget
  ),
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-card",
    style = card_style,
    section_head(
      "Students Below Grade Thresholds",
      "Counts of students finishing below 70%, 80%, and 90%, grouped by cohort."
    ),
    s3_widget
  ),
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-card",
    style = card_style,
    section_head(
      "Assessment Breakdown by Cohort",
      "Median score and N per assessment, pivoted across cohorts to compare item-level performance over time."
    ),
    s4_widget
  ),
  # Section 5 (Cohort Aggregate Statistics) intentionally removed —
  # duplicated the Overview cohort summary + Section 1 stats table.
)
```

# Curriculum Tag Coverage

```{r}
#| title: "Curriculum coverage"
# Full-width vertical stack — one section per tag set. Per spec:
#   1. Section heading card
#   2. Per-cohort coverage rows (colored dot + x/y tags + % + progress bar)
#   3. Grouped bar chart (tag counts per cohort)
#   4. Multi-column heatmap (one column per cohort)
#   5. Uncovered-tag pills with per-cohort dots (solid = covered, hollow = not)
#
# Implementation: ONE regular R chunk returning a nested tagList. No
# output: asis and no print() inside an asis block — widgets go through
# htmltools' knit_print which correctly registers plotly dependencies.

# Truncate long tag labels for chart display. Compresses well-known
# multi-line AVMA / EPA / PIG descriptors first, then hard-truncates
# anything still over max_chars. Vectorized over `label`.
truncate_tag_label <- function(label, max_chars = 45) {
  label <- as.character(label)
  label <- stringr::str_squish(label)

  # AVMA
  label <- gsub("Emergency and intensive care case management.*", "Emergency & ICU Case Management", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Basic medicine skills and case management.*", "Basic Medicine & Case Management", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Comprehensive patient diagnosis.*diagnostic testing.*record management.*", "Patient Diagnosis & Diagnostic Testing", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Comprehensive treatment planning.*referral.*", "Treatment Planning & Referral", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Basic surgery skills and case management.*", "Basic Surgery & Case Management", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Anesthesia and pain management.*welfare.*", "Anesthesia, Pain Management & Welfare", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Ethical and professional conduct.*communication.*diversity.*", "Ethics, Professionalism & Communication", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Understanding of health promotion.*biosecurity.*zoonoses.*food safety.*", "Health Promotion, Biosecurity & Zoonoses", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Critical Analysis of new information.*veterinary medicine.*", "Critical Analysis & Research", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)

  # EPAs
  label <- gsub("Develop and implement a management/treatment plan.*", "Management & Treatment Plan", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Recognize a patient requiring urgent or emergent care.*evaluation and management.*", "Urgent/Emergent Care & Management", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Formulate recommendations for preventive healthcare.*", "Preventive Healthcare", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub(".*zoonotic disease.*mitigate potential risks.*public health.*standard operating.*", "Zoonotic Risk & Public Health Protocols", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("aggression\\) and medical.*zoonotic.*public health.*hospital protocols.*", "Zoonotic Risk & Public Health Protocols", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Perform general anesthesia and recovery.*monitoring and support.*", "General Anesthesia & Recovery", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Perform a common surgical procedure.*pre-operative.*post-operative.*", "Surgical Procedure & Perioperative Care", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Formulate relevant questions and retrieve evidence.*", "Evidence-Based Practice", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Develop a diagnostic plan and interpret results.*", "Diagnostic Plan & Result Interpretation", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Gather a history, perform an examination.*differential diagnosis.*", "History, Exam & Differential Diagnosis", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Business acumen.*", "Business Acumen", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Ethical considerations.*Time management.*Self directed.*Career Planning.*", "Ethics, Time Management & Career Planning", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)

  # PIGs
  label <- gsub("discuss an appropriate Diagnostic Plan and Treatment.*", "Diagnostic Plan & Treatment/Management", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("appropriate for patient that is updated.*", "", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("in all aspects of veterinary medicine", "", label, ignore.case = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("with consideration of client obj.*", "", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("as new information develops", "", label, ignore.case = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("in professional and personal life", "", label, ignore.case = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("including food safety", "", label, ignore.case = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Establishes and develops their role within.*team approach.*", "Veterinary Health Care Team & Collaboration", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Analyzes and develops Preventive Care Plans.*herd health.*", "Preventive Care & Herd Health", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Recognize, discuss and address Zoonotic.*", "Zoonotic & Public Health", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Can apply Diversity and Inclusion.*", "Diversity, Inclusion & Communication", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Applies sound reasoning in Career.*", "Career & Personal Development", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Makes sound decisions about financial.*", "Financial & Business Decisions", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Can perform Sedation, Local Anesthesia.*", "Sedation & General Anesthesia", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  label <- gsub("Analyzes options for Pain Management.*", "Pain Management", label, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)

  # General cleanup
  label <- stringr::str_squish(label)
  ifelse(nchar(label) > max_chars,
         paste0(substr(label, 1, max_chars - 3), "..."),
         label)
}

if (length(tag_set_names) == 0) {
  ui_banner("No tag coverage data available for this course.", variant = "info")
} else {

  # -- htmltools-rendered coverage heatmap -----------------------------
  # The key accreditation visual. Pure HTML grid (no plotly) so:
  #   * cells are guaranteed ≥ 36px tall and show the count number directly
  #   * each column fills white → that cohort's assigned color at the
  #     column's own max (so the same count reads differently across
  #     cohorts by color, which is the point — per-column saturation)
  #   * printable/PDF-friendly: no canvas, no JS required to see it
  render_tag_heatmap <- function(cohort_counts, cohort_colors, all_tags) {
    if (length(all_tags) == 0 || length(cohort_counts) == 0) {
      return(tags$p(style = "color:#5A6472;margin:12px 0;",
                    "No data for heatmap."))
    }
    labels <- names(cohort_counts)
    # Column max for normalization (avoid 0 divide).
    col_max <- vapply(labels, function(lab) {
      vs <- cohort_counts[[lab]]
      m <- suppressWarnings(max(as.numeric(vs[names(vs) %in% all_tags]), na.rm = TRUE))
      if (!is.finite(m) || m <= 0) 1 else m
    }, numeric(1))

    header_row <- tags$div(
      style = sprintf(
        "display:grid;grid-template-columns:240px repeat(%d, 1fr);gap:2px;margin-bottom:2px;",
        length(labels)
      ),
      tags$div(style = "font-size:11px;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.05em;color:#5A6472;font-weight:600;padding:8px 4px;", "Tag"),
      lapply(labels, function(lab) {
        col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
        tags$div(
          style = sprintf("padding:10px 6px;text-align:center;font-size:11px;font-weight:700;color:#FFFFFF;background:%s;border-radius:6px 6px 0 0;letter-spacing:0.03em;", col),
          lab
        )
      })
    )

    body_rows <- lapply(all_tags, function(t) {
      tags$div(
        style = sprintf(
          "display:grid;grid-template-columns:240px repeat(%d, 1fr);gap:2px;margin-bottom:2px;",
          length(labels)
        ),
        tags$div(
          style = "padding:8px 6px;font-size:12px;color:#1A1A2E;min-height:36px;display:flex;align-items:center;border-right:1px solid #E0E4EC;",
          t
        ),
        lapply(labels, function(lab) {
          col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
          vs <- cohort_counts[[lab]]
          val <- if (t %in% names(vs)) as.numeric(vs[[t]]) else 0
          ratio <- if (is.finite(val) && val > 0) val / col_max[[lab]] else 0
          bg <- hex_rgba(col, ratio)
          # Text color flips to white once fill is dark enough.
          txt_col <- if (ratio > 0.55) "#FFFFFF" else "#1A1A2E"
          tags$div(
            style = sprintf(
              "min-height:36px;background:%s;color:%s;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;font-size:12px;font-weight:500;border:1px solid #E4E7EC;border-radius:4px;",
              bg, txt_col
            ),
            title = sprintf("%s — %s: %d", t, lab, as.integer(val)),
            if (val > 0) format(as.integer(val)) else "·"
          )
        })
      )
    })

    tags$div(
      style = "margin:8px 0 16px 0;overflow-x:auto;",
      header_row,
      body_rows
    )
  }

  # -- per-section builder ---------------------------------------------
  build_tagset_section <- function(ts) {
    pretty <- pretty_tagset_name(ts)

    cohort_counts <- stats::setNames(
      lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
        r <- all_data[[lab]]
        if (is.null(r$tag_coverage) || length(r$tag_coverage) == 0) return(integer(0))
        merged <- dplyr::bind_rows(Filter(Negate(is.null),
                                          lapply(r$tag_coverage, function(x) x[[ts]])))
        tagset_counts_vec(merged)
      }),
      names(all_data)
    )

    # Taxonomy-wide totals for the heading subtitle.
    all_tags_any <- unique(unlist(lapply(cohort_counts, names)))
    all_tags_any <- all_tags_any[!is_deprecated_tag(all_tags_any)]
    n_total_tags <- length(all_tags_any)
    n_any_cov <- sum(vapply(all_tags_any, function(t) {
      any(vapply(cohort_counts, function(cc) {
        v <- if (t %in% names(cc)) cc[[t]] else 0; v > 0
      }, logical(1)))
    }, logical(1)))

    coverage_row <- function(lab) {
      col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
      r <- all_data[[lab]]
      merged <- if (!is.null(r$tag_coverage) && length(r$tag_coverage) > 0) {
        dplyr::bind_rows(Filter(Negate(is.null),
                                lapply(r$tag_coverage, function(x) x[[ts]])))
      } else tibble::tibble()
      s <- tag_coverage_summary(merged)
      pct_display <- if (is.na(s$pct)) "—" else sprintf("%.1f%%", s$pct)
      pct_w <- if (is.na(s$pct)) 0 else max(0, min(100, as.numeric(s$pct)))
      # CRITICAL: use CSS GRID (not flex). Quarto auto-injects
      # `html-fill-container` onto every htmltools child, which sets
      # `flex: 1 1 auto` and overrides our per-child min-widths — making
      # flex siblings collapse to near-zero. CSS grid with explicit
      # column tracks is immune to that override.
      # Fix 2: per-spec 5-column grid — 20px dot | 180px name | 120px
      # "X / Y tags" | 60px percentage | 1fr progress bar. Gap 12px,
      # 10px vertical padding, 1px bottom border between rows.
      tags$div(
        class = "cvm-filterable",
        `data-cohort-labels` = lab,
        style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:20px 180px 120px 60px 1fr;align-items:center;gap:12px;padding:14px 0;border-bottom:1px solid #F0F0F0;",
        # col 1: colored dot (14px dot centered inside 20px track)
        tags$span(style = sprintf(
          "display:inline-block;width:14px;height:14px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;", col
        )),
        # col 2: cohort name
        tags$span(style = "font-weight:600;color:#003F6B;white-space:nowrap;", lab),
        # col 3: "X / Y tags"
        tags$span(style = "color:#5A6472;font-size:13px;white-space:nowrap;",
                  sprintf("%d / %d tags", s$covered, s$total)),
        # col 4: percentage (right-aligned inside its 60px cell)
        tags$span(style = sprintf("font-weight:600;color:%s;white-space:nowrap;text-align:right;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;", col),
                  pct_display),
        # col 5: progress bar fills remaining space
        tags$div(
          style = "background:#E8ECF0;height:10px;border-radius:999px;overflow:hidden;",
          tags$div(style = sprintf(
            "background:%s;height:100%%;width:%.1f%%;border-radius:999px;", col, pct_w))
        )
      )
    }

    # Package-style plot: dodged vertical bars, rotated x labels — matches
    # the look of cvm.course.portfolio::generate_tagset_plots() shipped in
    # the PPT export. Converted to plotly for interactivity.
    # Truncate long tag labels for chart readability — pure display
    # transform on the count vectors' names before they reach plotly.
    cohort_counts_display <- lapply(cohort_counts, function(cc) {
      if (is.null(cc) || length(cc) == 0) return(cc)
      stats::setNames(cc, truncate_tag_label(names(cc)))
    })
    bar <- tag_pkg_style_plotly_multi(
      cohort_counts_display, cohort_colors, title = pretty,
      height = max(600, 100 + 28 * length(all_tags_any))
    )
    bar$plot <- bar$plot |> plotly::layout(
      margin = list(l = 340, r = 40, t = 60, b = 60),
      yaxis = list(tickfont = list(size = 11), automargin = TRUE)
    )

    # Heatmap — rebuilt as htmltools grid (see render_tag_heatmap above).
    heatmap_ui <- render_tag_heatmap(cohort_counts, cohort_colors, all_tags_any)

    # Uncovered in EVERY cohort = uncovered_universe. The original helper
    # surfaced "uncovered in ANY cohort"; per spec tightened to "Not
    # covered in any cohort" so the panel is clearly a gap list.
    fully_uncovered <- all_tags_any[vapply(all_tags_any, function(t) {
      all(vapply(cohort_counts, function(cc) {
        v <- if (t %in% names(cc)) cc[[t]] else 0; v == 0
      }, logical(1)))
    }, logical(1))]

    uncovered_ui <- if (length(fully_uncovered) == 0) {
      tags$p(
        style = sprintf("color:%s;font-weight:500;margin:12px 0 0 0;", CVM_PALETTE$success),
        "Every tag is covered in at least one cohort."
      )
    } else {
      tagList(
        tags$p(
          style = "font-size:11px;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;color:#5A6472;font-weight:600;margin:16px 0 8px 0;",
          sprintf("Not covered in any cohort (%d)", length(fully_uncovered))
        ),
        tags$div(
          style = "display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:8px;margin-top:4px;",
          lapply(fully_uncovered, function(t) {
            tags$span(
              style = "display:inline-block;padding:6px 12px;border:1px solid #003F6B;border-radius:20px;color:#003F6B;background:#FFFFFF;font-size:12px;font-weight:500;white-space:nowrap;",
              t
            )
          })
        )
      )
    }

    # Fix 2 + Fix 4: the whole tag-set section is ONE white card per spec
    # (12px radius, subtle shadow, 24px padding). Gold 4px accent rule on
    # the left keeps the brand tag-coverage motif. Inside the card: a
    # compact header row (title + tag-count pill), then sub-sections
    # titled with `.cvm-card-title` (navy 16px/600).
    tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card",
      id = paste0("tagset-", tolower(gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "-", ts))),
      style = paste0(
        "width:100%;display:block;clear:both;",
        "margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;",
        "background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-left:4px solid #AB8B00;",
        "border-radius:12px;box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);scroll-margin-top:20px;"
      ),
      # Card header: tag-set title + muted subtitle + gold pill.
      tags$div(
        style = "display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;gap:16px;flex-wrap:wrap;margin-bottom:20px;",
        tags$div(
          tags$h2(
            class = "cvm-card-title",
            style = "font-size:22px; font-weight:800; color:#003F6B; border-bottom:3px solid #C9A84C; padding-bottom:12px; margin-bottom:20px;",
            pretty
          ),
          tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
                 sprintf("%d covered across all cohorts · %d total in taxonomy",
                         n_any_cov, n_total_tags))
        ),
        tags$span(
          style = "display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:6px;background:#FFF5E0;border:1px solid #E8D78A;color:#8A6D00;padding:6px 14px;border-radius:999px;font-size:13px;font-weight:600;letter-spacing:0.02em;",
          tags$strong(style = "color:#AB8B00;", n_total_tags),
          " tags"
        )
      ),
      tags$h3(class = "cvm-card-title", style = "font-size:14px;margin-top:8px;", "Disciplines covered by graduating class"),
      tags$div(
        style = "display:grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(160px, 1fr)); gap:12px; margin-top:16px;",
        lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
          col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
          r <- all_data[[lab]]
          merged <- if (!is.null(r$tag_coverage) && length(r$tag_coverage) > 0) {
            dplyr::bind_rows(Filter(Negate(is.null),
                                    lapply(r$tag_coverage, function(x) x[[ts]])))
          } else tibble::tibble()
          s <- tag_coverage_summary(merged)
          covered <- as.integer(s$covered %||% 0L)
          total   <- as.integer(s$total   %||% 0L)
          pct     <- if (is.na(s$pct)) 0 else as.numeric(s$pct)
          tags$div(
            class = "cvm-filterable",
            `data-cohort-labels` = lab,
            style = sprintf(
              "background:#FFFFFF; border:1px solid #E0E4EC; border-top:4px solid %s; border-radius:10px; padding:16px; text-align:center;",
              col
            ),
            tags$div(style = sprintf("width:12px;height:12px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;display:inline-block;margin-bottom:8px;", col)),
            tags$div(style = sprintf("font-weight:700; color:%s; font-size:13px; margin-bottom:6px;", col), lab),
            tags$div(style = "font-size:22px; font-weight:800; color:#003F6B; line-height:1;", sprintf("%d/%d", covered, total)),
            tags$div(style = "font-size:11px; color:#5A6472; margin-top:4px;", "tags covered"),
            tags$div(style = sprintf("font-size:18px; font-weight:700; color:%s; margin-top:8px;", col), sprintf("%.1f%%", pct)),
            tags$div(
              style = "background:#F0F0F0; border-radius:4px; height:5px; width:100%; margin-top:8px;",
              tags$div(style = sprintf("background:%s; border-radius:4px; height:5px; width:%.1f%%;", col, min(pct, 100)))
            )
          )
        })
      ),
      tags$h3(class = "cvm-card-title", style = "font-size:14px;margin-top:24px;", "Tag coverage by component"),
      tags$button(
        onclick = sprintf(
          "downloadTagsetCSV('%s', '%s')",
          htmlEscape(ts), htmlEscape(pretty)
        ),
        style = "float:right;margin:0 0 8px 0;padding:4px 10px;background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #C7CDD6;color:#003F6B;border-radius:6px;font-size:11px;font-weight:600;cursor:pointer;",
        "↓ CSV"
      ),
      ui_plot_box(bar$plot, height = max(600, 100 + 28 * length(all_tags_any))),
      ui_deprecated_note(bar$deprecated_count),
      uncovered_ui
    )
  }

  do.call(tagList, lapply(tag_set_names, build_tagset_section))
}
```

## Cross-Year Tag Signals

```{r}
#| title: "Cross-year tag signals"
# Tag-coverage signal stats — moved here from the Cross-Year Comparison tab
# so all tag-coverage analytics live under one heading. Renders the
# top 5 most consistently covered tags and the top 5 most variable tags
# (potential curriculum gaps) side-by-side. Requires ≥ 2 cohorts of data.

if (length(all_data) < 2 || length(tag_set_names) == 0) {
  ui_banner(
    "Cross-year tag signals require at least two cohorts with tag coverage data.",
    variant = "info"
  )
} else {
  cohort_counts_by_ts <- stats::setNames(
    lapply(tag_set_names, function(ts) {
      stats::setNames(
        lapply(all_data, function(r) {
          if (is.null(r$tag_coverage) || length(r$tag_coverage) == 0) return(integer(0))
          merged <- dplyr::bind_rows(Filter(Negate(is.null),
                                            lapply(r$tag_coverage, function(x) x[[ts]])))
          tagset_counts_vec(merged)
        }),
        names(all_data)
      )
    }),
    tag_set_names
  )
  tag_stats <- compute_tag_stats(cohort_counts_by_ts)
  top_c <- top_consistent_tags(tag_stats, 5)
  top_v <- top_variable_tags(tag_stats, 5)

  # Inner mini-cards — soft background + accent rule; sit inside the
  # parent .cvm-card so they intentionally have no drop shadow.
  tag_card <- function(title, df, metric_col, metric_label, accent) {
    tags$div(
      style = sprintf(
        "background:#F9FAFB;border-left:4px solid %s;border-radius:8px;padding:18px 20px;",
        accent
      ),
      tags$div(
        style = "font-size:11px;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;color:#5A6472;font-weight:600;margin-bottom:10px;",
        title
      ),
      if (nrow(df) == 0) {
        tags$p(style = "color:#5A6472;font-size:13px;margin:0;",
               "Not enough tag variation across cohorts to rank.")
      } else {
        tags$ol(
          style = "margin:0;padding-left:20px;",
          lapply(seq_len(nrow(df)), function(i) {
            row <- df[i, ]
            tags$li(
              style = "padding:4px 0;font-size:13px;color:#1A1A2E;",
              tags$strong(row$tag),
              tags$span(
                style = "color:#5A6472;font-size:11px;margin-left:6px;",
                sprintf("(%s · %s %s)",
                        pretty_tagset_name(row$tagset),
                        metric_label,
                        if (is.numeric(row[[metric_col]])) sprintf("%.1f", row[[metric_col]])
                        else as.character(row[[metric_col]]))
              )
            )
          })
        )
      }
    )
  }
  tag_cards_ui <- tags$div(
    style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:24px;margin-top:8px;",
    tag_card("Top 5 most consistently covered tags",
             top_c, "min", "min count",
             CVM_PALETTE$success),
    tag_card("Top 5 most variable tags (possible gaps)",
             top_v, "sd", "SD",
             CVM_PALETTE$warning)
  )

  cyts_card_style <- paste0(
    "width:100%;display:block;clear:both;",
    "margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;",
    "background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-radius:12px;",
    "box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);"
  )

  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-card", style = cyts_card_style,
    tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Tag-coverage signals"),
    tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
           "Which tags are covered every year vs. which shift cohort to cohort (potential curriculum gaps)."),
    tag_cards_ui
  )
}
```

# Cross-Year Comparison

```{r}
#| title: "Cross-year comparison"
# Redesigned as a set of analyses that mean something for curriculum
# evaluation (per spec):
#
#   1. Grade trajectory — per-cohort arrow vs. previous cohort, ± delta
#   2. Delivery consistency score — CV of medians → High / Medium / Low
#   3. Student volume trend — bar chart of N students per cohort
#   4. Top 5 most consistently covered tags (highest minimum count)
#   5. Top 5 most variable tags (curriculum-gap signals)
#   6. Assessment difficulty signal — per-assessment trend across cohorts,
#      decline > 5 points flagged
#
# All analyses come from helpers in R/comparison_engine.R so the same code
# powers the rule-based narrative and this tab.

if (length(all_data) < 2) {
  ui_banner("Only one cohort has data for this course — cross-year comparison requires two or more.", variant = "info")
} else {
  traj         <- compute_cohort_trajectory(all_data)
  consistency  <- compute_consistency_score(all_data)
  volume       <- compute_student_volume(all_data)
  assess_trend <- compute_assessment_trends(all_data, min_cohorts = 3)

  # Tag-coverage signal stats (top consistent / variable tags) moved to the
  # Curriculum Tag Coverage tab under "Cross-Year Tag Signals" — see the
  # chunk titled "Cross-year tag signals" lower in this template.

  # ---------- 1. Grade trajectory ----------
  # Trajectory now reports `% ≥ 80` as the primary metric — directly
  # measures competency and is unaffected by extra-credit-inflated weighted
  # formulas. Median is shown as a secondary line for context. Delta is in
  # percentage points (pp) vs the previous cohort. No more anomaly arrows /
  # scale warnings — the grade-capping note belongs in Assessment
  # Performance where raw grades are surfaced.
  trajectory_ui <- {
    rows <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(traj)), function(i) {
      row <- traj[i, ]
      col <- cohort_color_for(row$cohort)
      trend <- row$trend
      arrow <- switch(trend %||% "NA",
                      up      = "▲",
                      down    = "▼",
                      stable  = "▬",
                      "—")
      arrow_color <- switch(trend %||% "NA",
                            up      = CVM_PALETTE$success,
                            down    = CVM_PALETTE$warning,
                            stable  = CVM_PALETTE$muted,
                            CVM_PALETTE$muted)
      pct_text <- if (is.na(row$pct_80)) "—"
                  else sprintf("%.1f%% at ≥ 80", row$pct_80)
      med_text <- if (is.na(row$median)) ""
                  else sprintf("Median %.1f", row$median)
      delta_text <- if (is.na(row$delta)) {
        "(no prior cohort baseline)"
      } else {
        sprintf("%+.1fpp vs %s",
                row$delta,
                sub("^Class of ", "GC",
                    substr(row$prev_cohort,
                           nchar(row$prev_cohort) - 3,
                           nchar(row$prev_cohort))))
      }
      tags$div(
        style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:20px 180px 40px 1fr 1fr;align-items:center;gap:12px;padding:14px 0;border-bottom:1px solid #F0F0F0;",
        tags$span(style = sprintf(
          "display:inline-block;width:14px;height:14px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;", col
        )),
        tags$span(style = "font-weight:600;color:#003F6B;white-space:nowrap;", row$cohort),
        tags$span(style = sprintf("font-size:22px;font-weight:700;color:%s;text-align:center;", arrow_color), arrow),
        tags$div(
          tags$div(style = "font-size:18px;font-weight:700;color:#1A1A2E;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;",
                   pct_text),
          tags$div(style = "font-size:11px;color:#5A6472;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;",
                   med_text)
        ),
        tags$span(style = sprintf("font-size:12px;color:%s;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;",
                                  arrow_color),
                  delta_text)
      )
    })
    rows
  }

  # ---------- 2. Consistency score ----------
  cv_display <- if (is.null(consistency$cv) || is.na(consistency$cv)) "—"
                else sprintf("%.2f%%", consistency$cv)
  # Color semantics now match CV-direct labels: Low CV → green (stable),
  # Medium → amber, High → red (inconsistent). The labels match the
  # interpretation guide rendered below.
  consistency_bar <- switch(consistency$label,
                            Low    = CVM_PALETTE$success,
                            Medium = CVM_PALETTE$warning,
                            High   = CVM_PALETTE$alert,
                            CVM_PALETTE$muted)
  range_text <- if (is.finite(consistency$range_min %||% NA_real_) &&
                    is.finite(consistency$range_max %||% NA_real_)) {
    sprintf("%% ≥ 80 range: %.1f%% – %.1f%% across last %d cohorts",
            consistency$range_min, consistency$range_max,
            consistency$n_used)
  } else NULL

  consistency_ui <- tags$div(
    style = sprintf(
      "border-left:4px solid %s;padding:8px 0 8px 20px;display:flex;flex-direction:column;justify-content:flex-start;",
      consistency_bar
    ),
    tags$div(
      style = "font-size:11px;font-weight:600;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;color:#5A6472;margin-bottom:4px;",
      "Delivery Consistency (last 3 cohorts)"
    ),
    tags$div(
      style = "font-size:11px;color:#5A6472;margin-bottom:12px;font-style:italic;",
      "Based on % students achieving ≥ 80%"
    ),
    tags$div(
      style = sprintf("font-size:56px;font-weight:700;color:%s;line-height:1;letter-spacing:-0.02em;", consistency_bar),
      consistency$label
    ),
    tags$div(
      style = "font-size:13px;color:#1A1A2E;margin-top:10px;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;",
      sprintf("CV = %s", cv_display)
    ),
    if (!is.null(range_text)) {
      tags$div(
        style = "font-size:13px;color:#1A1A2E;margin-top:6px;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;",
        range_text
      )
    },
    tags$div(
      style = "margin-top:18px;padding:14px 16px;background:#F5F7FA;border-radius:8px;",
      tags$div(
        style = "font-size:11px;font-weight:600;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.06em;color:#5A6472;margin-bottom:8px;",
        "How to read this"
      ),
      tags$ul(
        style = "margin:0;padding-left:18px;font-size:12px;color:#5A6472;line-height:1.6;",
        tags$li(HTML("<strong>CV (Coefficient of Variation)</strong> = SD ÷ Mean × 100")),
        tags$li("Measures how consistently the course delivers outcomes year over year"),
        tags$li(HTML("<strong>Low CV (&lt; 5%)</strong> = stable delivery — outcomes are consistent across cohorts")),
        tags$li(HTML("<strong>Medium CV (5–15%)</strong> = some variation — worth monitoring")),
        tags$li(HTML("<strong>High CV (&gt; 15%)</strong> = significant year-to-year variation — review curriculum or delivery changes")),
        tags$li(HTML("The <strong>% ≥ 80 range</strong> shows the actual spread: e.g. 93.9%–99.5% means in the best year 99.5% of students passed at 80%+, in the worst year 93.9% did"))
      )
    )
  )

  # ---------- 3. Student volume ----------
  # Rendered as a compact DT table (Cohort | N Students | Periods) instead
  # of a bar chart — the values are short and a table reads cleaner here.
  volume_table_df <- data.frame(
    Cohort       = volume$cohort,
    `N Students` = as.integer(volume$students),
    Periods      = vapply(volume$cohort, function(lab) {
      r <- all_data[[lab]]
      as.integer(r$kpi$periods_covered %||% 0L)
    }, integer(1)),
    check.names      = FALSE,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  # Colored dot prefix on the Cohort cell so the table keeps the cohort
  # color motif used elsewhere on the page. escape=FALSE on the DT below.
  volume_table_df$Cohort <- vapply(volume_table_df$Cohort, function(lab) {
    col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
    sprintf('<span style="display:inline-block;width:10px;height:10px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;margin-right:8px;vertical-align:middle;"></span>%s',
            col, htmlEscape(lab))
  }, character(1), USE.NAMES = FALSE)

  volume_dt <- DT::datatable(
    volume_table_df,
    rownames = FALSE, escape = FALSE,
    options  = list(
      dom        = "t",
      ordering   = FALSE,
      pageLength = 20,
      paging     = FALSE,
      scrollX    = FALSE,
      columnDefs = list(
        list(targets = 0,   className = "dt-left"),
        list(targets = 1:2, className = "dt-right")
      ),
      initComplete = DT::JS(
        "function(settings, json) {",
        "  $(this.api().table().header()).find('th').css({",
        "    'background-color': '#003F6B',",
        "    'color':            '#FFFFFF',",
        "    'font-weight':      '600',",
        "    'border-bottom':    '3px solid #AB8B00'",
        "  });",
        "}"
      )
    )
  )

  # ---------- 6. Assessment difficulty signal ----------
  assess_ui <- if (nrow(assess_trend) == 0) {
    ui_banner(paste("No assessment has data in 3+ cohorts yet — difficulty",
                    "signals will become available as more cohorts finish."),
              variant = "info")
  } else {
    # Filter out rows whose assessment name is NA / blank / whitespace.
    df <- assess_trend |>
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(assessment) & stringr::str_squish(assessment) != "")

    if (nrow(df) == 0) {
      ui_banner(paste("No assessment has data in 3+ cohorts yet — difficulty",
                      "signals will become available as more cohorts finish."),
                variant = "info")
    } else {
      # Derive a cohort range string per assessment from all_data — purely a
      # display column. Each cohort label is shortened "Class of 2026" → "GC26"
      # and joined with " → " so the table cell reads e.g. "GC23 → GC24 → GC25 → GC26".
      assess_cohorts <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
        r <- all_data[[lab]]
        if (is.null(r$grades_df) || nrow(r$grades_df) == 0) return(NULL)
        non_final <- setdiff(unique(r$grades_df$assessment),
                             c("weighted_overall_grade",
                               "individual_weighted_overall"))
        if (length(non_final) == 0) return(NULL)
        data.frame(assessment = non_final, cohort = lab,
                   stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
      })) |>
        dplyr::arrange(.data$cohort) |>
        dplyr::group_by(.data$assessment) |>
        dplyr::summarise(`Cohort range` = paste(
          vapply(unique(.data$cohort), function(c) {
            paste0("GC", substr(c, nchar(c) - 1, nchar(c)))
          }, character(1)),
          collapse = " → "
        ), .groups = "drop")

      df <- df |> dplyr::left_join(assess_cohorts, by = "assessment")

      # Neutral, observational labels for the direction-of-change signal.
      df$Signal <- vapply(df$flag, function(f) {
        switch(f,
               declining = "Shift down",
               improving = "Shift up",
               stable    = "Stable",
               f)
      }, character(1))

      dt <- DT::datatable(
        df |>
          dplyr::transmute(
            Assessment      = .data$assessment,
            `Cohort range`  = .data$`Cohort range`,
            `First mean`    = .data$first_mean,
            `Last mean`     = .data$last_mean,
            Δ               = .data$delta,
            Signal          = .data$Signal
          ),
        rownames = FALSE, escape = FALSE,
        extensions = "Buttons",
        options  = list(
          dom = "Bfrtip", pageLength = 15, scrollX = FALSE,
          buttons = cvm_dt_buttons("assessment_trends"),
          columnDefs = list(
            list(targets = 0, className = "dt-left"),
            list(targets = 1,   className = "dt-left"),
            list(targets = 2:5, className = "dt-right")
          )
        )
      ) |>
        DT::formatStyle(
          "Signal",
          backgroundColor = DT::styleEqual(
            c("Shift down", "Shift up", "Stable"),
            c("#F5F6FA",   "#F5F6FA",  "#F5F6FA"))
        )

      n_shifted <- sum(abs(df$delta) >= 5, na.rm = TRUE)
      tagList(
        if (n_shifted > 0) {
          ui_banner(paste(
            "Assessments where mean scores have shifted by more than 5",
            "points between the earliest and latest cohort. Noted for",
            "review — changes may reflect curriculum evolution,",
            "assessment redesign, or cohort differences."
          ), variant = "info")
        },
        dt
      )
    }
  }

  # ---------- Assemble ----------
  # Fix 3 + Fix 4: every cross-year section is its own white .cvm-card
  # with 12px radius, subtle shadow, 24px padding. Titles use the navy
  # 16px/600 `.cvm-card-title`. Cards clear floats and block-level so
  # the four panels stack cleanly, zero overlap.
  cy_card_style <- paste0(
    "width:100%;display:block;clear:both;",
    "margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;",
    "background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-radius:12px;",
    "box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);"
  )
  tagList(
    # Card 1: Grade trajectory + Delivery-consistency pull-quote side by side.
    tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card", style = cy_card_style,
      tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Final Grade trajectory — cohort over cohort"),
      tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
             "Share of students at ≥ 80% per cohort, with delta vs the prior cohort; consistency verdict uses the last 3 cohorts."),
      tags$div(
        style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:2fr 1fr;gap:24px;align-items:start;",
        tags$div(trajectory_ui),
        tags$div(consistency_ui)
      )
    ),
    # Card 2: Student volume trend (compact table).
    tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card", style = cy_card_style,
      tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Student volume trend"),
      tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
             "Number of students assessed in each cohort and the number of curriculum periods covered."),
      volume_dt
    ),
    # Card 3: Assessment difficulty signal.
    tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card", style = cy_card_style,
      tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Assessment difficulty signal"),
      tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
             "Per-assessment mean change between earliest and latest cohort. Shifts of more than 5 points are noted for review."),
      assess_ui
    )
  )
}
```

# Survey Trends

```{r}
#| title: "Survey Trends"
# Multi-year survey trend tab.
#
# Data contract: one CSV per course at data/survey_responses/{SAFE_CODE}.csv
# with columns cohort, question_text, mean_score, n_respondents.
# (See data/survey_responses/README.md for the full schema.)
#
# Behavior:
#   * If a CSV exists matching the course code, render a plotly line
#     chart — one line per question, x = cohort year, y = mean score.
#   * Else, if any survey PDFs exist for any cohort, show a "data
#     available — extract responses" nudge.
#   * Else, show the instructional message only.

st_card_style <- paste0(
  "width:100%;display:block;clear:both;",
  "margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;",
  "background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-radius:12px;",
  "box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);"
)

safe_code_for_csv <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "", primary$course_code)
# CSV location: written by scripts/extract_survey_responses.R into the
# course's own outputs/ folder so the file ships alongside the rendered
# dashboard. Same folder Downloads/.render_cache live in — two levels
# above params$all_data_path.
course_dir_for_csv <- dirname(dirname(params$all_data_path))
survey_csv_path <- file.path(course_dir_for_csv, "data", "survey_responses",
                             paste0(safe_code_for_csv, ".csv"))
survey_csv_rel  <- sub("^.*/outputs/", "outputs/", survey_csv_path)

# Detect whether ANY surveys folder has at least one PDF.
any_survey_pdfs <- any(vapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
  r <- all_data[[lab]]
  s_dir <- surveys_dir(r$course_code, r$course_name, r$cohort_label)
  if (!dir.exists(s_dir)) return(FALSE)
  length(list.files(s_dir, pattern = "\\.pdf$")) > 0
}, logical(1)))

instructional_block <- tags$div(
  style = "padding:18px 22px;background:#EEF4FB;border-left:4px solid #003F6B;border-radius:8px;color:#003F6B;font-size:14px;line-height:1.6;",
  if (any_survey_pdfs) {
    HTML(paste0(
      "<strong>Survey data available</strong> — run the extraction script ",
      "to enable structured trend graphs. ",
      "Expected CSV at <code>", htmlEscape(survey_csv_rel), "</code> with columns ",
      "<code>cohort, question, response_option, count</code> ",
      "(produced by <code>scripts/extract_survey_responses.R</code>)."
    ))
  } else {
    HTML(paste0(
      "<strong>Survey trend chart not yet populated.</strong> ",
      "Run <code>scripts/extract_survey_responses.R</code> to produce ",
      "<code>", htmlEscape(survey_csv_rel), "</code> with columns ",
      "<code>cohort, question, response_option, count</code>, ",
      "then re-render this course to surface a multi-cohort response-distribution chart."
    ))
  }
)

# CSV branch — reads the extraction-script output (one row per
# cohort × question × response_option, with a `count`). Renders a stacked
# bar chart of Likert agreement distribution per cohort plus a per-cohort
# response-volume summary.
csv_branch <- function() {
  survey_df <- tryCatch(
    readr::read_csv(survey_csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  required_cols <- c("cohort", "question", "response_option", "count")
  if (is.null(survey_df) || !all(required_cols %in% names(survey_df))) {
    return(tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card", style = st_card_style,
      tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Survey Trends"),
      ui_banner("Survey CSV not found or malformed.", variant = "warn")
    ))
  }

  # Strip version suffix e.g. "Class of 2024 (v3)" -> "Class of 2024"
  survey_df$cohort <- stringr::str_replace(survey_df$cohort, "\\s*\\(v\\d+\\)\\s*$", "")
  survey_df$count  <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(survey_df$count))
  survey_df <- survey_df[!is.na(survey_df$count) & survey_df$count > 0, , drop = FALSE]

  if (nrow(survey_df) == 0) {
    return(tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card", style = st_card_style,
      tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Survey Trends"),
      ui_banner("Survey CSV has no valid rows.", variant = "warn")
    ))
  }

  # Cohort order
  cohort_year   <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(sub("^.*([0-9]{4}).*$", "\\1", survey_df$cohort)))
  cohort_levels <- unique(survey_df$cohort[order(cohort_year, survey_df$cohort)])

  # Classify questions
  survey_df$q_type <- dplyr::case_when(
    grepl("strength|well|positive|working|effective|good", survey_df$question, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "strengths",
    grepl("improve|weak|concern|challeng|issue|problem|need|area", survey_df$question, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "improvements",
    TRUE ~ "other"
  )

  # Drop NA / blank response options — these are unanswered questions,
  # not meaningful selections
  survey_df <- survey_df[
    !is.na(survey_df$response_option) &
    !grepl("^\\s*NA\\s*$|^\\s*N/A\\s*$|^\\s*$", survey_df$response_option, ignore.case = TRUE),
    , drop = FALSE
  ]

  make_freq_table <- function(df_sub, accent_color, top_n = 8) {
    if (nrow(df_sub) == 0) return(NULL)
    df_top <- df_sub[order(-df_sub$count), ][seq_len(min(top_n, nrow(df_sub))), ]
    total  <- sum(df_sub$count, na.rm = TRUE)
    df_top$pct <- round(100 * df_top$count / total, 1)
    tags$table(
      style = "width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:13px;margin-bottom:8px;",
      tags$thead(tags$tr(
        tags$th(style = "text-align:left;padding:4px 8px;color:#5A6472;font-weight:600;border-bottom:1px solid #EEF1F5;", "Response"),
        tags$th(style = "text-align:right;padding:4px 8px;color:#5A6472;font-weight:600;border-bottom:1px solid #EEF1F5;width:50px;", "N"),
        tags$th(style = "text-align:right;padding:4px 8px;color:#5A6472;font-weight:600;border-bottom:1px solid #EEF1F5;width:55px;", "%")
      )),
      tags$tbody(lapply(seq_len(nrow(df_top)), function(i) {
        row <- df_top[i, ]
        tags$tr(
          style = if (i %% 2 == 0) "background:#F9FAFB;" else "",
          tags$td(style = "padding:5px 8px;color:#1A1A2E;", row$response_option),
          tags$td(style = "padding:5px 8px;text-align:right;color:#1A1A2E;", row$count),
          tags$td(style = sprintf("padding:5px 8px;text-align:right;color:%s;font-weight:600;", accent_color),
                  paste0(row$pct, "%"))
        )
      }))
    )
  }

  cohort_cards <- lapply(cohort_levels, function(coh) {
    df_c   <- survey_df[survey_df$cohort == coh, ]
    str_df <- df_c[df_c$q_type == "strengths", ]
    imp_df <- df_c[df_c$q_type == "improvements", ]
    oth_df <- df_c[df_c$q_type == "other", ]
    dot_col <- cohort_color_for(coh)

    tags$div(
      style = "margin-bottom:28px;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-radius:10px;padding:20px;background:#FFFFFF;",
      tags$h3(
        style = "margin:0 0 14px 0;font-size:15px;font-weight:700;color:#1A1A2E;",
        tags$span(style = sprintf("display:inline-block;width:10px;height:10px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;margin-right:8px;vertical-align:middle;", dot_col)),
        coh
      ),
      if (nrow(str_df) > 0) tagList(
        tags$p(style = "font-size:11px;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;color:#2E7D32;font-weight:700;margin:0 0 6px 0;", "Top strengths"),
        make_freq_table(str_df, "#2E7D32")
      ),
      if (nrow(imp_df) > 0) tagList(
        tags$p(style = "font-size:11px;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;color:#C62828;font-weight:700;margin:14px 0 6px 0;", "Areas for improvement"),
        make_freq_table(imp_df, "#C62828")
      ),
      if (nrow(oth_df) > 0 && nrow(str_df) == 0 && nrow(imp_df) == 0) tagList(
        tags$p(style = "font-size:11px;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;color:#5A6472;font-weight:700;margin:0 0 6px 0;", "Responses"),
        make_freq_table(oth_df, "#5A6472")
      )
    )
  })

  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-card", style = st_card_style,
    tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Survey Trends"),
    tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
           "End-of-course survey response frequencies by cohort. Top 8 items shown per question type. Source: structured CSV from scripts/extract_survey_responses.R."),
    do.call(tagList, cohort_cards)
  )
}

pdf_branch <- function() {
  records <- analyse_survey_trends(all_data, cohort_colors)
  if (length(records) == 0) {
    return(tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card", style = st_card_style,
      tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Survey Trends"),
      tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
             "Multi-year mean response per survey question, once data is available."),
      instructional_block
    ))
  }

  cohort_order <- vapply(records, function(rec) rec$cohort, character(1))

  # Keyword agreement summary feeds the headline insight card only.
  # The aggregate trend pivot table was removed in favor of stacked
  # per-cohort response cards that are easier to scan side-by-side.
  trend_df <- build_survey_trend_summary(records)
  cohort_cols <- intersect(cohort_order, names(trend_df) %||% character())

  # ── Summary insight card ──────────────────────────────────────────
  summary_card <- {
```
