```{r}
    if (is.null(trend_df) || nrow(trend_df) == 0 || length(cohort_cols) == 0) NULL
    else {
      latest_col <- cohort_cols[[length(cohort_cols)]]
      latest_vals <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(trend_df[[latest_col]]))
      strong   <- trend_df$keyword[!is.na(latest_vals) & latest_vals >= 75]
      weak     <- trend_df$keyword[!is.na(latest_vals) & latest_vals < 50]
      improving <- trend_df$keyword[trend_df$trend == "improving"]
      declining <- trend_df$keyword[trend_df$trend == "declining"]

      bullet_line <- function(color, label, items) {
        if (length(items) == 0) return(NULL)
        tags$li(
          style = "margin-bottom:8px;color:#1A1A2E;",
          tags$strong(style = sprintf("color:%s;", color), paste0(label, ": ")),
          paste(items, collapse = "; ")
        )
      }
      bullets <- tags$ul(
        style = "margin:0;padding-left:20px;font-size:14px;line-height:1.7;",
        bullet_line("#2E7D32", "Strong agreement (≥ 75)", strong),
        bullet_line("#C62828", "Low agreement (< 50)",     weak),
        bullet_line("#2E7D32", "Improving across cohorts", improving),
        bullet_line("#F57C00", "Declining across cohorts", declining)
      )
      tags$div(
        class = "cvm-card", style = st_card_style,
        tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "What the surveys are telling us"),
        tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
               sprintf("Across %d cohort%s (latest: %s).",
                       length(cohort_cols),
                       if (length(cohort_cols) == 1) "" else "s",
                       latest_col)),
        bullets
      )
    }
  }

  # ── Per-cohort STRUCTURED response tables ────────────────────────
  # Inline parser: walk the raw PDF text line-by-line and pick out rows
  # of the form
  #
  #   <component label>   SA  A  N  D  SD  [N/A]  Total
  #
  # validating that Total ≈ sum of the categorical counts. Returns a
  # data.frame with Agree / Neutral / Disagree percentages, the base N,
  # and a colored Rating chip. Returns NULL when no rows can be
  # confidently extracted, so the per-cohort card can swap to a clean
  # one-line fallback message instead of dumping raw text.
  parse_likert_rows <- function(text) {
    if (is.null(text) || !is.character(text) || !nzchar(text)) return(NULL)
    lines <- unlist(strsplit(text, "\n", fixed = TRUE))
    # cvm.gradebook survey export PDF row format (verified against the
    # 6 cohort PDFs):
    #
    #   "N. <label>   SA  A  N  D  SD  [NA-col]   <NA>/<Total>   Avg"
    #
    # The integer between SD and the "/" can be a true N/A column OR can
    # already be the X in "X / Y" — the PDF column widths shift between
    # rows. Rather than hardcode column positions we:
    #   1. scan each line for the "X / Y" score pattern; Y is the total
    #   2. strip the score and the trailing decimal (Average) tokens
    #   3. tokenize the remaining ints; the first 5 are SA, A, N, D, SD
    #      (we ignore any extra ints in between — they're either the N/A
    #      column duplicate or the X-in-score, neither of which we need)
    #   4. base = SA+A+N+D+SD; N column = max(base, total_from_score)
    out <- list()
    for (line in lines) {
      line <- trimws(line)
      if (!nzchar(line)) next
      # Skip percentage rows ("69.86% 30.14% ..."), header continuations
      # ("Strongly agree"), and any line we recognize as non-data.
      if (grepl("%", line, fixed = TRUE)) next
      if (grepl("^(Strongly|Somewhat|Neither|Aggregate|Score|Average|Total|Form:|Report|Completed|Question|Section|Course)\\b",
                line, ignore.case = TRUE)) next

      # Score column "X / Y" → total_from_score = Y.
      score_m <- regmatches(line, regexec("(\\d+)\\s*/\\s*(\\d+)",
                                          line, perl = TRUE))[[1]]
      total_from_score <- if (length(score_m) >= 3) as.integer(score_m[[3]]) else NA_integer_
      stripped <- if (length(score_m) > 0) {
        sub("\\d+\\s*/\\s*\\d+", " ", line, perl = TRUE)
      } else line
      # Drop trailing decimal (Average column).
      stripped <- sub("\\s+\\d+\\.\\d+\\s*$", "", stripped, perl = TRUE)
      # Drop a leading "N." item numbering.
      stripped <- sub("^(\\d+)\\.\\s+", "", stripped, perl = TRUE)

      ints <- regmatches(stripped,
                         gregexpr("\\b\\d+\\b", stripped, perl = TRUE))[[1]]
      if (length(ints) < 5) next
      ints_n <- as.integer(ints)
      sa <- ints_n[[1]]; a  <- ints_n[[2]]; n <- ints_n[[3]]
      d  <- ints_n[[4]]; sd <- ints_n[[5]]
      answered <- sa + a + n + d + sd
      if (answered <= 0) next
      total <- if (!is.na(total_from_score) && total_from_score >= answered) {
        total_from_score
      } else answered

      label <- trimws(sub("\\s+\\d.*$", "", stripped, perl = TRUE))
      if (nchar(label) < 3 || nchar(label) > 120) next
      # Skip rows whose "label" is actually a histogram bin caption
      # (e.g. "[0-5] (5-15] (15-25] ... Missing") — those crop up when a
      # cohort's surveys/ folder also contains a feedback-report PDF
      # rather than an end-of-course Likert PDF. Heuristic: a real
      # course-component label has at least 8 alphabetic characters and
      # no square-bracket/parenthesis-numeric tokens.
      letters_only <- gsub("[^A-Za-z]", "", label)
      if (nchar(letters_only) < 8) next
      if (grepl("[\\[\\(]\\s*\\d", label, perl = TRUE)) next

      out[[length(out) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Component       = label,
        `Agree (%)`     = round(100 * (sa + a) / answered, 1),
        `Neutral (%)`   = round(100 *  n        / answered, 1),
        `Disagree (%)`  = round(100 * (sd + d) / answered, 1),
        N               = total,
        check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
    if (length(out) == 0) return(NULL)
    do.call(rbind, out)
  }

  # Each cohort gets its OWN white card with cohort-colored header — no
  # rating chips, no aggregate trend table. Just the four-column response
  # breakdown so faculty can scan the numbers directly. The wrapper
  # `cvm-survey-cohort-card` enforces clear:both + display:block + 32 px
  # bottom margin so cards never overlap.
  per_cohort_cards <- lapply(records, function(rec) {
    src <- rec$raw_text %||% rec$text %||% ""
    parsed <- parse_likert_rows(src)

    # Narrative fallback: when the PDF is a feedback-report style document
    # (no Likert rows parse cleanly), parse_survey_pdf() leaves a
    # `key_findings` entry of type "narrative" with extracted sentences.
    # Render those as a clean bullet list so the cohort card has content
    # instead of just an instructional message.
    narr_q  <- rec$questions[["key_findings"]]
    is_narr <- !is.null(narr_q) &&
               identical(narr_q$type %||% NA_character_, "narrative") &&
               length(narr_q$sentences %||% character(0)) > 0

    body <- if (!is.null(parsed)) {
      DT::datatable(
        parsed, rownames = FALSE, escape = FALSE,
        extensions = "Buttons",
        options = list(
          dom = "Bfrtip", pageLength = 25, scrollX = FALSE, ordering = TRUE,
          buttons = cvm_dt_buttons(sprintf("survey_%s",
                                           gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_",
                                                rec$cohort))),
          columnDefs = list(
            list(targets = 0, className = "dt-left", width = "300px"),
            list(targets = 1:3, className = "dt-right"),
            list(targets = 4,   className = "dt-right", width = "60px")
          ),
          initComplete = DT::JS(
            "function(settings, json) {",
            "  $(this.api().table().header()).find('th').css({",
            "    'background-color': '#003F6B',",
            "    'color':            '#FFFFFF',",
            "    'font-weight':      '600'",
            "  });",
            "}"
          )
        )
      )
    } else if (is_narr) {
      tagList(
        tags$p(
          style = "margin:0 0 12px 0;font-size:13px;color:#5A6472;font-style:italic;",
          "Narrative-format report — key sentences extracted:"
        ),
        tags$ul(
          style = "margin:0;padding-left:20px;font-size:14px;line-height:1.65;color:#1A1A2E;",
          lapply(as.character(narr_q$sentences), function(s) {
            tags$li(style = "margin-bottom:8px;", s)
          })
        )
      )
    } else {
      tags$p(
        style = "margin:0;font-size:13px;color:#5A6472;",
        sprintf("Survey PDF available — provide structured data at data/survey_responses/%s.csv for detailed analysis.",
                safe_code_for_csv)
      )
    }

    tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card cvm-survey-cohort-card",
      style = sprintf(
        paste0("background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-left:6px solid %s;",
               "border-radius:12px;padding:24px;",
               "margin-bottom:32px !important;display:block !important;clear:both !important;",
               "box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);"),
        rec$color
      ),
      tags$h2(
        class = "cvm-card-title",
        style = sprintf("color:%s;margin-top:0;", rec$color),
        rec$cohort
      ),
      tags$p(
        class = "cvm-card-desc",
        style = "font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums;",
        sprintf("Source: %s", htmlEscape(rec$pdf))
      ),
      body
    )
  })

  # ── Data-quality note ────────────────────────────────────────────
  note_card <- tags$div(
    class = "cvm-card", style = st_card_style,
    tags$div(
      style = "padding:14px 18px;background:#FFF8E1;border-left:4px solid #AB8B00;border-radius:8px;color:#5A4A00;font-size:13px;line-height:1.6;",
      HTML(sprintf(
        paste0("<strong>Note:</strong> these scores are estimated from PDF text extraction ",
               "and are intended for trend signals only. For precise analysis, ",
               "provide structured CSV data at <code>data/survey_responses/%s.csv</code>."),
        htmlEscape(safe_code_for_csv)
      ))
    )
  )

  do.call(tagList, c(list(summary_card), per_cohort_cards, list(note_card)))
}

if (file.exists(survey_csv_path)) {
  csv_branch()
} else {
  pdf_branch()
}
```

# Course Insights

```{r}
narr_path <- sub("\\.rds$", "_narrative.rds", params$all_data_path)
narrative <- if (file.exists(narr_path)) readRDS(narr_path) else NULL

if (is.null(narrative)) {
  ui_banner("Course narrative not generated for this render.", variant = "info")
} else if (isFALSE(narrative$available %||% TRUE)) {
  HTML(paste0(
    '<div class="cvm-narrative"><h3>Course narrative not available</h3><p>',
    htmlEscape(narrative$narrative),
    '</p></div>'
  ))
} else {
  # Flag cards — icon + colored left border + title + description.
  sev_palette <- list(
    high   = list(bar = "#C62828", bg = "#FFEBEE", text = "#8E1A1A", icon = "!", label = "HIGH PRIORITY"),
    medium = list(bar = "#F57C00", bg = "#FFF3E0", text = "#8A4A00", icon = "!", label = "MEDIUM PRIORITY"),
    low    = list(bar = "#2E7D32", bg = "#E8F5E9", text = "#1B5E20", icon = "✓", label = "LOW PRIORITY")
  )
  flags_ui <- if (!is.null(narrative$flags) && nrow(narrative$flags) > 0) {
    tagList(lapply(seq_len(nrow(narrative$flags)), function(i) {
      f <- narrative$flags[i, ]
      p <- sev_palette[[f$severity]] %||% sev_palette$medium
      tags$div(
        style = sprintf(
          "display:grid;grid-template-columns:32px 1fr;column-gap:14px;align-items:start;padding:16px 20px;margin-bottom:12px;background:%s;border-left:4px solid %s;border-radius:8px;",
          p$bg, p$bar
        ),
        # Circular icon badge
        tags$span(
          style = sprintf(
            "width:28px;height:28px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;color:#FFFFFF;display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;font-weight:700;font-size:14px;",
            p$bar
          ),
          p$icon
        ),
        tags$div(
          tags$div(
            style = sprintf("font-size:10px;font-weight:700;letter-spacing:0.1em;color:%s;margin-bottom:4px;", p$bar),
            p$label
          ),
          tags$div(
            style = sprintf("font-size:14px;font-weight:600;color:%s;margin-bottom:6px;line-height:1.4;", p$text),
            f$issue
          ),
          tags$div(
            style = sprintf("font-size:13px;color:%s;line-height:1.5;opacity:0.85;", p$text),
            f$recommendation
          )
        )
      )
    }))
  } else NULL

  # Questions — numbered cards. CSS grid for the inner layout so Quarto's
  # html-fill-container can't collapse the question text to 0 width.
  questions_ui <- if (length(narrative$questions) > 0) {
    tags$div(
      style = "display:grid;row-gap:10px;",
      lapply(seq_along(narrative$questions), function(i) {
        q <- narrative$questions[[i]]
        tags$div(
          style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:32px 1fr;column-gap:14px;align-items:start;padding:14px 18px;background:#EEF4FB;border-left:3px solid #003F6B;border-radius:8px;",
          tags$span(
            style = "width:28px;height:28px;border-radius:50%;background:#003F6B;color:#FFFFFF;display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;font-weight:700;font-size:13px;",
            paste0("Q", i)
          ),
          tags$div(
            style = "font-size:13px;color:#1A1A2E;line-height:1.55;word-break:normal;",
            q
          )
        )
      })
    )
  } else NULL

  # Distinguish rule-based vs LLM — the former gets a "Generated from data"
  # badge; the latter keeps the model attribution.
  source_kind <- narrative$source %||% "llm"
  badge <- if (source_kind == "rule-based") {
    tags$span(
      style = "display:inline-block;padding:4px 10px;background:#EEF4FB;color:#003F6B;font-size:11px;font-weight:600;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;border-radius:999px;border:1px solid #CDE0ED;",
      "Generated from data"
    )
  } else {
    tags$span(
      style = "display:inline-block;padding:4px 10px;background:#FFF5E0;color:#8A6D00;font-size:11px;font-weight:600;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;border-radius:999px;border:1px solid #E8D78A;",
      "Model-generated"
    )
  }
  footer_line <- if (source_kind == "rule-based") {
    "Narrative is computed from the data. It does not substitute for faculty review."
  } else {
    sprintf("Generated %s using %s.",
            fmt_ts(narrative$generated_at),
            narrative$model %||% "—")
  }

  # Fix 4: each insight block is a white card — narrative, flags, and
  # questions each live in their own .cvm-card. Footer attribution sits
  # below the last card as muted microcopy.
  ci_card_style <- paste0(
    "width:100%;display:block;clear:both;",
    "margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;",
    "background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-radius:12px;",
    "box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);"
  )
  tagList(
    tags$button(
      onclick = "window.print()",
      style = "margin-bottom:16px;padding:8px 16px;background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #003F6B;color:#003F6B;border-radius:6px;font-size:13px;font-weight:600;cursor:pointer;",
      "🖨 Print / Save as PDF"
    ),
    tags$div(
      class = "cvm-card cvm-narrative",
      style = ci_card_style,
      tags$div(
        style = "display:flex;justify-content:flex-end;margin-bottom:12px;",
        badge
      ),
      HTML(markdown_to_html(narrative$narrative))
    ),
    if (!is.null(flags_ui)) {
      tags$div(
        class = "cvm-card", style = ci_card_style,
        tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Areas of concern"),
        tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
               "Issues ranked by severity with specific faculty follow-up recommendations."),
        flags_ui
      )
    },
    if (FALSE) {  # HIDDEN — Questions to explore
      # Re-enable by changing FALSE to TRUE.
      # Server-side guard so the section never reaches the HTML — CSS
      # display:none was unreliable in some Quarto/bslib layouts.
      if (!is.null(questions_ui)) {
        tags$div(
          class = "cvm-card cvm-questions-explore", style = ci_card_style,
          tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Questions to explore"),
          tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
                 "Prompts to carry into the next faculty curriculum-review conversation."),
          questions_ui
        )
      }
    },
    tags$p(
      style = "color:#5A6472;font-size:12px;margin:-24px 0 32px 0;padding:0 4px;",
      footer_line
    )
  )
}
```

<!--
HIDDEN — Downloads tab is hidden via CSS in R/layout_fixes.R
Re-enable by removing the CSS block tagged "Downloads tab hidden"
when Yashvi has added the survey PDFs to the outputs folders.
-->
# Downloads

```{r}
# Filter-aware Downloads tab.
#
# - Export Artifacts: ONE compact card listing PPTX + Excel if present;
#   a single-line info banner if not.
# - Per-cohort Survey cards: each wrapped in .cvm-filterable so the chip
#   filter bar shows only cards for currently selected cohorts. Cohort
#   label as a colored card header; PDF rows compact inside.
# - Compact banners (not full-height) for empty states.
#
# params$all_data_path points at .render_cache/all_years_result.rds;
# the course folder (where PPTX/XLSX land) is two levels up.
course_dir <- dirname(dirname(params$all_data_path))

pptx_files <- list.files(course_dir, pattern = "\\.pptx$",          full.names = TRUE)
xlsx_files <- list.files(course_dir, pattern = "_workbooks\\.zip$", full.names = TRUE)

has_exports <- length(pptx_files) > 0 || length(xlsx_files) > 0

dl_card_style <- paste0(
  "width:100%;display:block;clear:both;",
  "margin-bottom:48px;padding:24px;",
  "background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EEF1F5;border-radius:12px;",
  "box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);"
)

# ---- General Downloads (drop-anywhere sink) ---------------------------
# Anything Ish drops into outputs/{course}/downloads/ surfaces here.
# Auto-detected on render; no schema, no naming convention.
dl_dir_abs       <- downloads_dir(primary$course_code, primary$course_name)
dl_drop_path_rel <- sub("^.*/outputs/", "outputs/", dl_dir_abs)

icon_for_ext <- function(ext) {
  switch(tolower(ext),
         "pdf"  = "📄",
         "pptx" = "📊", "ppt" = "📊",
         "xlsx" = "📈", "xls" = "📈", "csv" = "📈",
         "zip"  = "🗜️",
         "doc"  = "📝", "docx" = "📝",
         "png"  = "🖼️", "jpg" = "🖼️", "jpeg" = "🖼️",
         "📁")
}

downloads_files <- if (dir.exists(dl_dir_abs)) {
  fs <- list.files(dl_dir_abs, full.names = TRUE, recursive = FALSE,
                   include.dirs = FALSE)
  # Skip dotfiles like .DS_Store.
  fs[!grepl("^\\.", basename(fs))]
} else character(0)

downloads_card <- tags$div(
  class = "cvm-card",
  style = dl_card_style,
  tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Downloads"),
  tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
         "Files dropped into this course's downloads folder are listed here automatically — any file type."),
  if (length(downloads_files) == 0) {
    tags$p(
      style = "margin:0;font-size:13px;color:#5A6472;",
      "No files available."
    )
  } else {
    tags$div(
      style = "display:grid;gap:10px;",
      lapply(downloads_files, function(p) {
        ext <- tools::file_ext(p)
        ui_download_row(
          label      = if (nzchar(ext)) toupper(ext) else "File",
          href       = file.path("downloads", basename(p)),
          filename   = basename(p),
          size_bytes = file.info(p)$size,
          icon       = icon_for_ext(ext)
        )
      })
    )
  }
)

exports_card <- tags$div(
  class = "cvm-card",
  style = dl_card_style,
  tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Export artifacts"),
  tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
         "Generated PowerPoint and Excel workbooks for this course portfolio."),
  if (has_exports) {
    tags$div(
      style = "display:grid;gap:10px;",
      if (length(pptx_files) > 0) {
        ui_download_row("PowerPoint", basename(pptx_files[[1]]),
                        basename(pptx_files[[1]]),
                        file.info(pptx_files[[1]])$size)
      },
      if (length(xlsx_files) > 0) {
        ui_download_row("Excel workbooks (zip)", basename(xlsx_files[[1]]),
                        basename(xlsx_files[[1]]),
                        file.info(xlsx_files[[1]])$size)
      }
    )
  } else {
    tags$p(
      style = "margin:0;font-size:13px;color:#5A6472;",
      "No exports generated yet."
    )
  }
)

# Per-cohort survey cards — one .cvm-filterable card per cohort.
# Placeholder-driven: when no PDF is present, show the exact drop path
# Ish needs to copy a file into. When PDFs are present, render compact
# download rows. No "ready_on / 2 days after end" microcopy anymore.
survey_cards <- lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
  r <- all_data[[lab]]
  col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
  safe_code <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "", r$course_code)
  class_slug_local <- cohort_slug(r$cohort_label)
  survey_path <- file.path("surveys", class_slug_local)

  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-filterable",
    `data-cohort-labels` = lab,
    style = sprintf(
      "display:block !important;background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #E0E4EC;border-left:4px solid %s;border-radius:12px;padding:20px 24px;",
      col
    ),
    # Header — colored dot + cohort name in cohort color.
    tags$div(
      style = "display:flex;align-items:center;gap:10px;margin-bottom:16px;",
      tags$span(style = sprintf(
        "display:inline-block;width:14px;height:14px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;", col
      )),
      tags$span(style = sprintf("font-size:16px;font-weight:700;color:%s;", col), lab)
    ),
    # Body — placeholder when no PDFs present; download rows otherwise.
    {
      s_dir <- surveys_dir(r$course_code, r$course_name, r$cohort_label)
      # Show only the most recently modified PDF per cohort. Older copies
      # stay in the folder for audit trail but the card surfaces the
      # canonical "current" report.
      pdfs <- if (dir.exists(s_dir)) {
        all_pdfs <- list.files(s_dir, pattern = "\\.pdf$", full.names = TRUE)
        if (length(all_pdfs) > 1) {
          all_pdfs[which.max(file.mtime(all_pdfs))]
        } else all_pdfs
      } else character(0)
      if (length(pdfs) == 0) {
        tags$div(
          style = "background:#F5F6FA;border-radius:8px;padding:14px 16px;",
          tags$div(
            style = "font-size:12px;color:#5A6472;margin-bottom:6px;",
            "No survey file yet. Drop PDF here:"
          ),
          tags$code(
            style = "font-size:11px;color:#003F6B;background:#EEF4FB;padding:4px 8px;border-radius:4px;display:block;",
            sprintf("outputs/%s_Foundations/surveys/%s/", safe_code, class_slug_local)
          )
        )
      } else {
        tags$div(
          style = "display:grid;gap:8px;",
          lapply(pdfs, function(p) {
            ui_download_row(
              label      = "Survey PDF",
              href       = file.path(survey_path, basename(p)),
              filename   = basename(p),
              size_bytes = file.info(p)$size
            )
          })
        )
      }
    }
  )
})

# Surveys section: one outer .cvm-card containing a responsive grid of
# per-cohort sub-cards (each still .cvm-filterable). The outer card gives
# the section a navy 16px title and consistent chrome; the inner cards
# carry the cohort-colored left border.
surveys_card <- tags$div(
  class = "cvm-card",
  style = dl_card_style,
  tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Course surveys by cohort"),
  tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
         "End-of-course survey PDFs available for each graduating class."),
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-survey-grid",
    style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(420px,1fr));gap:20px;",
    survey_cards
  )
)

# ---- Student Feedback section ----------------------------------------
# Per-cohort cards listing mid-course and delta feedback PDFs. The two
# folders are auto-created at render time. Drop-here message shown when
# the folder exists but is empty (parity with surveys).
feedback_cards <- lapply(names(all_data), function(lab) {
  r <- all_data[[lab]]
  col <- cohort_color_for(lab)
  safe_code <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "", r$course_code)
  class_slug_local <- cohort_slug(r$cohort_label)

  card_header <- tags$div(
    style = "display:flex;align-items:center;gap:10px;padding-bottom:12px;margin-bottom:14px;border-bottom:1px solid #E0E4EC;",
    tags$span(style = sprintf(
      "display:inline-block;width:14px;height:14px;border-radius:50%%;background:%s;", col
    )),
    tags$span(style = sprintf("font-size:16px;font-weight:700;color:%s;", col), lab)
  )

  build_kind_block <- function(kind, kind_label) {
    fb_dir_abs <- feedback_dir(r$course_code, r$course_name, r$cohort_label, kind)
    # New layout: feedback PDFs live in the same course folder.
    rel_base <- file.path("feedback", class_slug_local, kind)
    drop_path_rel <- sub("^.*/outputs/", "outputs/", fb_dir_abs)

    body <- if (!dir.exists(fb_dir_abs)) {
      tags$p(
        style = "margin:0;font-size:12px;color:#5A6472;",
        "Folder will be created on next render."
      )
    } else {
      pdfs <- list.files(fb_dir_abs, pattern = "\\.pdf$", full.names = TRUE)
      if (length(pdfs) == 0) {
        tags$p(
          style = "margin:0;font-size:12px;color:#5A6472;",
          HTML(sprintf("Folder ready — drop %s PDFs at <code>%s</code>",
                       kind_label, htmlEscape(drop_path_rel)))
        )
      } else {
        tags$div(
          style = "display:grid;gap:8px;",
          lapply(pdfs, function(p) {
            ui_download_row(
              label      = kind_label,
              href       = file.path(rel_base, basename(p)),
              filename   = basename(p),
              size_bytes = file.info(p)$size
            )
          })
        )
      }
    }
    tags$div(
      style = "margin-bottom:14px;",
      tags$div(
        style = "font-size:11px;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.08em;color:#5A6472;font-weight:600;margin-bottom:6px;",
        kind_label
      ),
      body
    )
  }

  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-filterable",
    `data-cohort-labels` = lab,
    style = sprintf(
      "display:block !important;background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #E0E4EC;border-left:4px solid %s;border-radius:12px;padding:20px 24px;box-shadow:0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);",
      col
    ),
    card_header,
    build_kind_block("midcourse", "Mid-course feedback PDF"),
    build_kind_block("delta",     "Delta feedback PDF")
  )
})

feedback_card <- tags$div(
  class = "cvm-card",
  style = dl_card_style,
  tags$h2(class = "cvm-card-title", "Student Feedback"),
  tags$p(class = "cvm-card-desc",
         "Mid-course and delta (end-of-course) student feedback PDFs by cohort. Drop PDFs into the folders shown below; they appear here automatically on the next render."),
  tags$div(
    class = "cvm-survey-grid",
    style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(420px,1fr));gap:20px;",
    feedback_cards
  )
)

tagList(downloads_card, exports_card, surveys_card, feedback_card)
```

```{r}
#| include: false
#| output: false
# Filter-bar JSON payload — next chunk writes the raw HTML block.
```

```{r}
#| include: false
# Feedback widget configuration — Google Form submission.
# Submitting to a Google Form requires no API key and no special sheet
# permissions: the widget POSTs to the form's formResponse endpoint with
# the entry.<id> field names that Google assigns to each question. Pull
# the entry IDs from a "Get pre-filled link" preview of the form.
#
# Env vars (set in ~/.Renviron):
#   CVM_FEEDBACK_FORM_ID            – the long ID between /forms/d/e/ and /viewform
#   CVM_FEEDBACK_ENTRY_TAB          – entry.NNNNNNNNNN for the tab dropdown
#   CVM_FEEDBACK_ENTRY_COURSE       – entry.NNNNNNNNNN for course code
#   CVM_FEEDBACK_ENTRY_FEEDBACK     – entry.NNNNNNNNNN for the free-text field
#   CVM_FEEDBACK_ENTRY_TIMESTAMP    – entry.NNNNNNNNNN for the timestamp field
feedback_form_id      <- Sys.getenv("CVM_FEEDBACK_FORM_ID",        unset = "")
feedback_entry_tab    <- Sys.getenv("CVM_FEEDBACK_ENTRY_TAB",      unset = "")
feedback_entry_course <- Sys.getenv("CVM_FEEDBACK_ENTRY_COURSE",   unset = "")
feedback_entry_fb     <- Sys.getenv("CVM_FEEDBACK_ENTRY_FEEDBACK", unset = "")
feedback_entry_ts     <- Sys.getenv("CVM_FEEDBACK_ENTRY_TIMESTAMP", unset = "")
feedback_enabled <- nzchar(feedback_form_id) && nzchar(feedback_entry_tab)
feedback_config_json <- jsonlite::toJSON(list(
  form_id         = feedback_form_id,
  entry_tab       = feedback_entry_tab,
  entry_course    = feedback_entry_course,
  entry_feedback  = feedback_entry_fb,
  entry_timestamp = feedback_entry_ts,
  course_code     = primary$course_code,
  enabled         = feedback_enabled
), auto_unbox = TRUE)
```

```{=html}
<script id="cvm-dashboard-data" type="application/json">
`r period_index_json`
</script>
<script id="cvm-cohort-colors" type="application/json">
`r cohort_colors_json`
</script>
<script id="cvm-course-code" type="application/json">
"`r primary$course_code`"
</script>
<script id="cvm-feedback-config" type="application/json">
`r feedback_config_json`
</script>
<script id="cvm-tag-data" type="application/json">
`r tag_data_json`
</script>
<style>
  /* Sticky filter bar — chunkier, more obviously interactive. The label
     is now stacked above the chip row, the chips are larger, and active
     chips fill solid navy with a 4px cohort-colored left edge so they
     clearly stand out from the inactive (light gray) chips. A 2px navy
     separator line sits below the bar to mark the visual break before
     the first content card. */
  .cvm-filter-bar {
    position: sticky;
    top: 0;
    z-index: 100;
    background: #FFFFFF;
    box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.08);
    padding: 14px 24px 18px 24px;
    display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: stretch; gap: 8px;
    border-bottom: 2px solid #003F6B;
    font-family: Inter, "Source Sans Pro", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
  }
  .cvm-filter-bar .label {
    font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700;
    text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em;
    color: #003F6B;
  }
  .cvm-filter-bar .chips {
    display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 10px;
    align-items: center;
  }
  .cvm-chip {
    display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;
    padding: 8px 18px;
    border: 2px solid #C7CDD6;
    border-left: 4px solid #C7CDD6;
    border-radius: 22px;
    background: #F5F7FA;
    color: #5A6472;
    font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600;
    cursor: pointer; user-select: none;
    transition: background 140ms, color 140ms, border-color 140ms;
  }
  .cvm-chip .chip-dot {
    display: inline-block;
    width: 10px; height: 10px;
    border-radius: 50%;
    box-sizing: border-box;
    background: transparent;
    border: 2px solid #9AA5B1;
  }
  .cvm-chip:hover {
    background: #E8ECF0;
    border-color: #9AA5B1;
    color: #1A1A2E;
  }
  .cvm-chip.active {
    background: #003F6B;
    color: #FFFFFF;
    border-color: #003F6B;
    border-left-color: var(--chip-color, #AB8B00);
    box-shadow: 0 2px 6px rgba(0, 63, 107, 0.25);
  }
  .cvm-chip.active .chip-dot {
    background: var(--chip-color, #FFFFFF);
    border-color: #FFFFFF;
  }
  .cvm-chip:focus-visible {
    outline: 2px solid #AB8B00;
    outline-offset: 2px;
  }
  .cvm-filter-actions {
    display: flex; gap: 14px; margin-left: auto;
  }
  @media (max-width: 700px) {
    .cvm-filter-actions { margin-left: 0; }
  }
  .cvm-filter-actions { margin-left: auto; display: flex; gap: 14px; }
  .cvm-filter-actions a {
    color: #AB8B00; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500;
    cursor: pointer; text-decoration: none;
    border-bottom: 1px dashed rgba(171,139,0,0.4);
  }
  .cvm-filter-actions a:hover { color: #003F6B; border-color: #003F6B; }
  .cvm-filter-actions a:focus-visible {
    outline: 2px solid #AB8B00; outline-offset: 2px;
  }
  .cvm-filterable { transition: opacity 150ms; }

  /* --- Fix 1: histogram grid — 3-column responsive layout, cards size to
     content so charts are never clipped. min-height gives a visual floor
     without imposing an overflow:hidden ceiling. */
  .cvm-histogram-grid {
    display: grid !important;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr) !important;
    grid-auto-rows: auto !important;
    gap: 16px !important;
  }
  .cvm-histogram-grid > .cvm-histogram-card {
    position: relative !important;
    min-height: 500px !important;
    height: auto !important;
    overflow: visible !important;
    margin: 0 !important;
  }

  /* --- Fix 4: Course Insights narrative — never clip; paragraphs breathe. */
  .cvm-narrative {
    overflow-y: visible !important;
    height: auto !important;
    max-height: none !important;
  }
  .cvm-narrative p { margin-bottom: 16px; }

  /* --- Design pass (Brand Guardian / UI Designer / UX Architect):
     Single card token reused across every tab's content sections.
     White background, 12px radius, subtle shadow, 24px padding,
     full-width block that clears floats so nothing overlaps. */
  .cvm-card {
    width: 100% !important;
    display: block !important;
    clear: both !important;
    box-sizing: border-box;
    background: #FFFFFF;
    border: 1px solid #EEF1F5;
    border-radius: 12px;
    box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.07);
    padding: 24px;
    margin-bottom: 48px;
  }
  .cvm-card > :first-child { margin-top: 0; }
  .cvm-card > :last-child  { margin-bottom: 0; }

  /* Section title + description inside a card. Navy 16px/600 per spec. */
  .cvm-card-title {
    font-family: Inter, "Source Sans Pro", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
    font-size: 16px;
    font-weight: 600;
    color: #003F6B;
    margin: 0 0 6px 0;
    letter-spacing: -0.005em;
  }
  .cvm-card-desc {
    font-size: 13px;
    color: #5A6472;
    margin: 0 0 20px 0;
    line-height: 1.5;
  }

  /* --- Numeric tables (Visual Storyteller / UI Designer):
     Navy header, alternating #F9FAFB row shading, right-aligned numbers,
     consistent padding. Scoped under `.cvm-card` so it never touches
     Overview. */
  .cvm-card table.dataTable thead th {
    background-color: #003F6B !important;
    color: #FFFFFF !important;
    font-weight: 600 !important;
    border-bottom: 0 !important;
    letter-spacing: 0.01em;
  }
  .cvm-card table.dataTable tbody tr:nth-child(even) > td {
    background-color: #F9FAFB !important;
  }
  .cvm-card table.dataTable tbody td { border-top: 1px solid #F0F2F5; padding: 10px 12px; }
  .cvm-card table.dataTable td.dt-right,
  .cvm-card table.dataTable th.dt-right { text-align: right; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

  /* --- Charts (Visual Storyteller): 380px floor, 16px internal padding,
     no clipping. Applies to plotly + htmlwidget containers inside cards. */
  .cvm-card .plotly.html-widget,
  .cvm-card .html-widget,
  .cvm-chart {
    min-height: 380px !important;
    padding: 16px;
    box-sizing: border-box;
  }

  /* --- Tab-pane hygiene: each redesigned tab scrolls vertically on long
     content so cards never clip under the navbar. Overview is explicitly
     excluded — per directive, do not touch it. */
  .tab-pane#assessment-performance,
  .tab-pane#curriculum-tag-coverage,
  .tab-pane#cross-year-comparison,
  .tab-pane#survey-trends,
  .tab-pane#course-insights,
  .tab-pane#downloads {
    overflow-y: auto !important;
    height: auto !important;
  }

  /* --- Feedback widget (fixed bottom-right, every tab) --------------- */
  .cvm-feedback-button {
    position: fixed;
    bottom: 24px;
    right: 24px;
    z-index: 9999;
    background: #003F6B;
    color: #FFFFFF;
    border: none;
    border-radius: 999px;
    padding: 12px 20px;
    font-size: 13px;
    font-weight: 600;
    letter-spacing: 0.02em;
    cursor: pointer;
    box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,0.18);
    font-family: Inter, "Source Sans Pro", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
  }
  .cvm-feedback-button:hover { background: #002D4D; }
  .cvm-feedback-button:focus-visible {
    outline: 2px solid #AB8B00; outline-offset: 2px;
  }
  .cvm-feedback-panel {
    position: fixed;
    bottom: 80px;
    right: 24px;
    z-index: 9999;
    width: 340px;
    max-width: calc(100vw - 48px);
    background: #FFFFFF;
    border: 1px solid #E0E4EC;
    border-radius: 12px;
    box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.18);
    padding: 18px 20px;
    font-family: Inter, "Source Sans Pro", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
    display: none;
  }
  .cvm-feedback-panel.open { display: block; }
  .cvm-feedback-panel h3 {
    margin: 0 0 12px 0;
    font-size: 15px;
    font-weight: 700;
    color: #003F6B;
  }
  .cvm-feedback-panel label {
    display: block;
    font-size: 11px;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 0.06em;
    color: #5A6472;
    font-weight: 600;
    margin-bottom: 4px;
    margin-top: 10px;
  }
  .cvm-feedback-panel select,
  .cvm-feedback-panel textarea {
    width: 100%;
    box-sizing: border-box;
    border: 1px solid #C7CDD6;
    border-radius: 6px;
    padding: 8px 10px;
    font-size: 13px;
    font-family: inherit;
    color: #1A1A2E;
    background: #FFFFFF;
  }
  .cvm-feedback-panel textarea {
    resize: vertical;
    min-height: 80px;
  }
  .cvm-feedback-panel select:focus,
  .cvm-feedback-panel textarea:focus {
    outline: 2px solid #003F6B; outline-offset: 1px;
    border-color: #003F6B;
  }
  .cvm-feedback-panel .cvm-fb-actions {
    display: flex;
    justify-content: space-between;
    align-items: center;
    margin-top: 14px;
    gap: 10px;
  }
  .cvm-feedback-panel .cvm-fb-submit {
    background: #003F6B;
    color: #FFFFFF;
    border: none;
    border-radius: 6px;
    padding: 8px 16px;
    font-size: 13px;
    font-weight: 600;
    cursor: pointer;
  }
  .cvm-feedback-panel .cvm-fb-submit:hover { background: #002D4D; }
  .cvm-feedback-panel .cvm-fb-submit:disabled {
    background: #9AA5B1; cursor: not-allowed;
  }
  .cvm-feedback-panel .cvm-fb-cancel {
    background: transparent;
    color: #5A6472;
    border: none;
    font-size: 12px;
    cursor: pointer;
  }
  .cvm-feedback-status {
    margin-top: 10px;
    font-size: 12px;
    line-height: 1.4;
  }
  .cvm-feedback-status.ok    { color: #2E7D32; }
  .cvm-feedback-status.err   { color: #C62828; }
  .cvm-feedback-status.info  { color: #5A6472; }

  /* DT Buttons (CSV / Excel) — compact icon-style row aligned right
     above each table. Keeps download chrome out of the table body so the
     data carries the visual weight (Brand Guardian / UI Designer). */
  .cvm-card .dt-buttons {
    float: right !important;
    margin: 0 0 12px 0 !important;
    display: flex !important;
    gap: 6px !important;
  }
  .cvm-card .dt-buttons .dt-button {
    background: #FFFFFF !important;
    border: 1px solid #C7CDD6 !important;
    color: #003F6B !important;
    border-radius: 6px !important;
    font-size: 11px !important;
    font-weight: 600 !important;
    padding: 4px 10px !important;
    line-height: 1.4 !important;
    box-shadow: none !important;
  }
  .cvm-card .dt-buttons .dt-button:hover {
    background: #EEF4FB !important;
    border-color: #003F6B !important;
  }
  /* Clear the float so the table sits properly below. */
  .cvm-card .dataTables_wrapper::after {
    content: ""; display: block; clear: both;
  }

  /* Print mode — hides chrome that doesn't make sense on paper and lets
     the narrative card lay out as one continuous block per page. The
     "Print / Save as PDF" button on Course Insights triggers this via
     window.print(). */
  @media print {
    .cvm-filter-bar, .cvm-feedback-button, .cvm-feedback-panel,
    .quarto-dashboard .navbar { display: none !important; }
    .cvm-card { break-inside: avoid; }
    .cvm-narrative { display: block !important; }
  }
</style>
<script>
(function() {
  "use strict";

  // ---- payload ----------------------------------------------------------
  function parseScript(id) {
    const el = document.getElementById(id);
    if (!el) return null;
    try { return JSON.parse(el.textContent); }
    catch (e) { console.error("cvm filter: could not parse", id, e); return null; }
  }
  const PERIODS = parseScript("cvm-dashboard-data") || [];
  const COHORT_COLORS = parseScript("cvm-cohort-colors") || {};
  const COURSE_CODE = parseScript("cvm-course-code") || "default";

  // PERIODS may be per-period (one entry per cperiod_id). Roll up to cohorts.
  // Each cohort is the filter unit; each period knows its cohort.
  const COHORTS = [];
  const seen = new Set();
  PERIODS.forEach(function(p) {
    if (!p || !p.cohort_label) return;
    if (seen.has(p.cohort_label)) return;
    seen.add(p.cohort_label);
    COHORTS.push({
      label: p.cohort_label,
      slug:  p.cohort_slug,
      chip:  (p.chip_label || p.label || p.cohort_label),
      color: COHORT_COLORS[p.cohort_label] || "#003F6B"
    });
  });
  // Ensure consistent chronological order (alphabetic on Class of YYYY).
  COHORTS.sort(function(a, b) { return a.label < b.label ? -1 : 1; });

  if (COHORTS.length === 0) {
    console.warn("cvm filter: no cohorts — filter bar not initialized");
    return;
  }

  // ---- state ------------------------------------------------------------
  const STORAGE_KEY = "cvm-filter-" + COURSE_CODE;
  const ALL_LABELS = COHORTS.map(function(c) { return c.label; });
  let active;
  try {
    const saved = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY) || "null");
    active = Array.isArray(saved)
      ? new Set(saved.filter(function(l) { return ALL_LABELS.indexOf(l) >= 0; }))
      : new Set(ALL_LABELS);
    if (active.size === 0) active = new Set(ALL_LABELS);
  } catch (e) { active = new Set(ALL_LABELS); }

  function persist() {
    try { localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(Array.from(active))); }
    catch (e) { /* private mode — ignore */ }
  }

  // ---- helpers ----------------------------------------------------------
  function median(xs) {
    if (xs.length === 0) return null;
    const s = xs.slice().sort(function(a, b) { return a - b; });
    const m = Math.floor(s.length / 2);
    return s.length % 2 ? s[m] : (s[m - 1] + s[m]) / 2;
  }

  // selectedPeriods = per-period rows whose cohort is active.
  function selectedPeriods() {
    return PERIODS.filter(function(p) { return active.has(p.cohort_label); });
  }

  function recomputeKpis() {
    const sel = selectedPeriods();
    const medians = sel.map(function(p) { return p.median; })
                       .filter(function(v) { return typeof v === "number"; });
    const students = sel.reduce(function(s, p) { return s + (p.students || 0); }, 0);
    const periods  = sel.length;
    const tagSets  = sel.reduce(function(m, p) {
      return Math.max(m, p.n_tag_sets || 0);
    }, 0);

    setKpi("median",   medians.length ? median(medians).toFixed(1) : "—");
    setKpi("students", students);
    setKpi("periods",  periods);
    setKpi("tag_sets", tagSets);
  }
  function setKpi(key, val) {
    document.querySelectorAll('[data-kpi="' + key + '"] .cvm-kpi-value').forEach(function(el) {
      el.textContent = String(val);
    });
  }

  function apply() {
    // Chip visual state
    document.querySelectorAll(".cvm-chip").forEach(function(chip) {
      const on = active.has(chip.dataset.cohortLabel);
      chip.classList.toggle("active", on);
      chip.setAttribute("aria-pressed", on ? "true" : "false");
    });
    // Filterable content blocks — data-cohort-labels is a comma-separated
    // list of cohort labels. Cohort labels contain spaces (e.g. "Class of
    // 2023") so we MUST split only on commas. Splitting on whitespace
    // shreds labels into ["Class","of","2023"] and hides every block.
    document.querySelectorAll(".cvm-filterable").forEach(function(el) {
      const raw = el.dataset.cohortLabels || "";
      const labels = raw.split(",").map(function(s) { return s.trim(); }).filter(Boolean);
      const match = labels.length === 0 ? true : labels.some(function(l) { return active.has(l); });
      // Use setProperty with "important" so we can beat any inline
      // `display:... !important` (e.g. Fix 5 survey cards). Removing the
      // property on match lets the element fall back to its natural
      // `display` — whether that's block, grid-item, flex-item, etc.
      if (match) {
        el.style.removeProperty("display");
      } else {
        el.style.setProperty("display", "none", "important");
      }
    });
    recomputeKpis();
    applyToCharts();
    applyToTables();

    // Collapse empty grid space — when the filter narrows the visible
    // card count, drop from a 3-column to 2- or 1-column layout so the
    // remaining cards stretch to fill the row instead of leaving gaps.
    document.querySelectorAll(".cvm-histogram-grid, .cvm-survey-grid").forEach(function(grid) {
      var visible = grid.querySelectorAll('.cvm-filterable:not([style*="display: none"])').length;
      if (visible === 1) {
        grid.style.gridTemplateColumns = "1fr";
      } else if (visible === 2) {
        grid.style.gridTemplateColumns = "repeat(2, 1fr)";
      } else {
        grid.style.gridTemplateColumns = "repeat(3, 1fr)";
      }
    });
  }

  // applyToTables: for every DataTable on the page, walk its rows and
  // hide any row whose visible text references a cohort label that's
  // NOT in the active set. Rows with no cohort reference (headers,
  // aggregates, summary lines) always stay visible.
  function applyToTables() {
    if (typeof window.jQuery === "undefined") return;
    var $ = window.jQuery;
    if (!$.fn || !$.fn.DataTable) return;
    var cohortLabels = Object.keys(COHORT_COLORS || {});
    if (cohortLabels.length === 0) return;
    document.querySelectorAll(".dataTable").forEach(function(table) {
      var dtInstance;
      try { dtInstance = $(table).DataTable(); }
      catch (e) { return; }
      if (!dtInstance) return;
      try {
        dtInstance.rows().every(function() {
          var node = this.node();
          if (!node) return;
          var rowText = node.textContent || node.innerText || "";
          var rowCohort = cohortLabels.find(function(c) {
            return rowText.indexOf(c) >= 0;
          });
          // No cohort label found anywhere in the row → header / summary.
          if (!rowCohort) { $(node).show(); return; }
          if (active.has(rowCohort)) $(node).show();
          else                        $(node).hide();
        });
        dtInstance.draw(false);
      } catch (e) {
        console.warn("cvm filter: applyToTables row walk failed", e);
      }
    });
  }

  // applyToCharts: re-runs every chip toggle (and on initial load via the
  // apply() call below). For every plotly chart on the page, walk its
  // traces and flip visibility based on whether the trace's name matches
  // an active cohort label. Non-cohort traces (e.g. the dashed gold "75%
  // reference" line, or any trace whose name doesn't appear in our cohort
  // color table) stay visible at all times. Cohort traces that belong to
  // an INACTIVE cohort are demoted to plotly's "legendonly" state — they
  // disappear from the chart but remain clickable in the legend, so a
  // user can still pop them back without re-toggling the chip.
  function applyToCharts() {
    if (typeof Plotly === "undefined") return;
    const cohortLabels = Object.keys(COHORT_COLORS || {});
    document.querySelectorAll(".js-plotly-plot").forEach(function(plot) {
      if (!plot.data || plot.data.length === 0) return;
      const update = { visible: [] };
      plot.data.forEach(function(trace) {
        const traceName = trace.name || "";
        // Is this trace tied to a known cohort?
        const isCohortTrace = cohortLabels.some(function(c) {
          return traceName === c || traceName.indexOf(c) >= 0;
        });
        if (!isCohortTrace) {
          update.visible.push(true);
          return;
        }
        const isActive = Array.from(active).some(function(cohort) {
          return traceName === cohort || traceName.indexOf(cohort) >= 0;
        });
        update.visible.push(isActive ? true : "legendonly");
      });
      try { Plotly.restyle(plot, update); } catch (e) {
        console.warn("cvm filter: Plotly.restyle failed", e);
      }
    });
  }

  // ---- build the bar ----------------------------------------------------
  function build() {
    const nav = document.querySelector(".quarto-dashboard .navbar")
             || document.querySelector("header.navbar")
             || document.querySelector("nav");
    if (!nav) { console.warn("cvm filter: no navbar anchor found"); return; }
    if (document.querySelector(".cvm-filter-bar")) return;  // already built

    const bar = document.createElement("div");
    bar.className = "cvm-filter-bar";
    bar.setAttribute("role", "toolbar");
    bar.setAttribute("aria-label", "Filter by graduating class");

    const label = document.createElement("div");
    label.className = "label";
    label.textContent = "Filter by cohort";
    bar.appendChild(label);

    // Sub-row holding chips on the left and actions on the right.
    const subRow = document.createElement("div");
    subRow.style.display = "flex";
    subRow.style.flexWrap = "wrap";
    subRow.style.alignItems = "center";
    subRow.style.gap = "10px";
    bar.appendChild(subRow);

    const chipsWrap = document.createElement("div");
    chipsWrap.className = "chips";
    COHORTS.forEach(function(c) {
      const chip = document.createElement("button");
      chip.type = "button";
      chip.className = "cvm-chip";
      chip.dataset.cohortLabel = c.label;
      chip.style.setProperty("--chip-color", c.color);
      chip.setAttribute("aria-pressed", "false");
      chip.setAttribute("aria-label", "Toggle " + c.label);
      const dot = document.createElement("span");
      dot.className = "chip-dot";
      chip.appendChild(dot);
      const txt = document.createElement("span");
      txt.textContent = c.chip;
      chip.appendChild(txt);
      chip.addEventListener("click", function() {
        if (active.has(c.label)) active.delete(c.label);
        else active.add(c.label);
        if (active.size === 0) active = new Set(ALL_LABELS);
        persist(); apply();
      });
      chipsWrap.appendChild(chip);
    });
    subRow.appendChild(chipsWrap);

    const actions = document.createElement("div");
    actions.className = "cvm-filter-actions";
    const selAll = document.createElement("a");
    selAll.textContent = "Select all";
    selAll.setAttribute("role", "button");
    selAll.setAttribute("tabindex", "0");
    selAll.addEventListener("click", function() { active = new Set(ALL_LABELS); persist(); apply(); });
    const clear = document.createElement("a");
    clear.textContent = "Clear";
    clear.setAttribute("role", "button");
    clear.setAttribute("tabindex", "0");
    clear.addEventListener("click", function() {
      active = new Set();
      persist(); apply();
      // 3s safety — reset to all if still empty.
      setTimeout(function() {
        if (active.size === 0) { active = new Set(ALL_LABELS); persist(); apply(); }
      }, 3000);
    });
    actions.appendChild(selAll); actions.appendChild(clear);
    subRow.appendChild(actions);

    nav.parentNode.insertBefore(bar, nav.nextSibling);
  }

  // ---- init -------------------------------------------------------------
  let booted = false;
  function runOnce() {
    if (!booted) {
      booted = true;
      try { build(); } catch (e) { console.error("cvm filter: build failed", e); }
    }
    try {
      apply();
      console.log("cvm filter: apply OK, " + COHORTS.length + " cohorts, active=" + active.size);
    } catch (e) { console.error("cvm filter: apply failed", e); }
  }
  if (document.readyState === "loading") {
    document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", runOnce);
  } else {
    runOnce();
  }
  window.addEventListener("load", runOnce);
  setTimeout(runOnce, 300);
})();
</script>
<script>
// downloadTagsetCSV — bound to the global scope so the inline onclick
// handler on each heatmap's "Download CSV" button can call it. Reads
// the per-tagset payload from <script id="cvm-tag-data"> emitted at
// render time and synthesizes a CSV file in the browser.
window.CVM_TAG_DATA = (function() {
  try {
    var el = document.getElementById("cvm-tag-data");
    if (!el) return {};
    return JSON.parse(el.textContent) || {};
  } catch (e) { console.warn("CVM_TAG_DATA parse failed", e); return {}; }
})();
window.downloadTagsetCSV = function(tagset, label) {
  var data = window.CVM_TAG_DATA && window.CVM_TAG_DATA[tagset];
  if (!data) { alert("No data available for " + label); return; }
  var cohorts = Object.keys(data);
  var tags = [];
  cohorts.forEach(function(c) {
    Object.keys(data[c]).forEach(function(t) {
      if (tags.indexOf(t) < 0) tags.push(t);
    });
  });
  var rows = [["Tag"].concat(cohorts)];
  tags.forEach(function(t) {
    var row = [t];
    cohorts.forEach(function(c) { row.push(data[c][t] || 0); });
    rows.push(row);
  });
  var csv = rows.map(function(r) {
    return r.map(function(v) {
      return '"' + String(v).replace(/"/g, '""') + '"';
    }).join(",");
  }).join("\n");
  var blob = new Blob([csv], { type: "text/csv" });
  var url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
  var a = document.createElement("a");
  a.href = url; a.download = label + "_coverage.csv";
  document.body.appendChild(a); a.click();
  document.body.removeChild(a); URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
};
</script>
<script>
// --- Feedback widget (every tab) -----------------------------------------
// Fixed bottom-right button that expands to a small form. On submit, the
// values are POSTed to a Google Form's formResponse endpoint via a hidden
// HTML form targeting an off-screen iframe. No API key and no sheet
// permissions are required — Google Forms accepts public submissions by
// design — and CORS is dodged because the submission is a same-document
// HTML form post, not a fetch().
(function() {
  "use strict";

  function readJson(id) {
    var el = document.getElementById(id);
    if (!el) return null;
    try { return JSON.parse(el.textContent); }
    catch (e) { console.error("feedback widget: parse failed for", id, e); return null; }
  }
  var CFG = readJson("cvm-feedback-config") || {};
  var TABS = ["Overview", "Assessment Performance", "Curriculum Tag Coverage",
              "Cross-Year Comparison", "Course Insights", "Downloads",
              "Survey Trends"];

  function submitFeedback(tabName, courseCode, feedbackText) {
    var FORM_ID         = CFG.form_id         || "";
    var ENTRY_TAB       = CFG.entry_tab       || "";
    var ENTRY_COURSE    = CFG.entry_course    || "";
    var ENTRY_FEEDBACK  = CFG.entry_feedback  || "";
    var ENTRY_TIMESTAMP = CFG.entry_timestamp || "";

    if (!FORM_ID || !ENTRY_TAB) {
      return Promise.reject(new Error("Google Form not configured"));
    }

    var formUrl = "https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/" + FORM_ID + "/formResponse";
    var params = new URLSearchParams();
    params.set(ENTRY_TAB, tabName);
    if (ENTRY_COURSE)    params.set(ENTRY_COURSE,    courseCode);
    if (ENTRY_FEEDBACK)  params.set(ENTRY_FEEDBACK,  feedbackText);
    if (ENTRY_TIMESTAMP) params.set(ENTRY_TIMESTAMP, new Date().toISOString());

    var iframe = document.createElement("iframe");
    iframe.style.display = "none";
    iframe.name = "cvm-fb-target-" + Date.now();
    document.body.appendChild(iframe);

    var form = document.createElement("form");
    form.method = "POST";
    form.action = formUrl;
    form.target = iframe.name;
    form.style.display = "none";

    params.forEach(function(val, key) {
      var input = document.createElement("input");
      input.type = "hidden";
      input.name = key;
      input.value = val;
      form.appendChild(input);
    });

    document.body.appendChild(form);
    form.submit();

    setTimeout(function() {
      try { document.body.removeChild(form); }   catch (e) {}
      try { document.body.removeChild(iframe); } catch (e) {}
    }, 3000);

    return Promise.resolve();
  }

  function buildButton() {
    if (document.querySelector(".cvm-feedback-button")) return;
    var btn = document.createElement("button");
    btn.type = "button";
    btn.className = "cvm-feedback-button";
    btn.textContent = "Give Feedback";
    btn.setAttribute("aria-label", "Open feedback form");
    btn.addEventListener("click", togglePanel);
    document.body.appendChild(btn);
  }

  function buildPanel() {
    if (document.querySelector(".cvm-feedback-panel")) return;
    var panel = document.createElement("div");
    panel.className = "cvm-feedback-panel";
    panel.setAttribute("role", "dialog");
    panel.setAttribute("aria-label", "Submit feedback");

    var h = document.createElement("h3"); h.textContent = "Share feedback";
    panel.appendChild(h);

    // Group all interactive form fields under .cvm-fb-fields so we can
    // hide them en masse when swapping to the thank-you state.
    var fieldsWrap = document.createElement("div");
    fieldsWrap.className = "cvm-fb-fields";
    panel.appendChild(fieldsWrap);

    var lblTab = document.createElement("label");
    lblTab.textContent = "Which tab are you on?";
    lblTab.setAttribute("for", "cvm-fb-tab");
    fieldsWrap.appendChild(lblTab);

    var sel = document.createElement("select");
    sel.id = "cvm-fb-tab";
    TABS.forEach(function(t) {
      var opt = document.createElement("option");
      opt.value = t; opt.textContent = t;
      sel.appendChild(opt);
    });
    fieldsWrap.appendChild(sel);

    var lblFb = document.createElement("label");
    lblFb.textContent = "Your feedback";
    lblFb.setAttribute("for", "cvm-fb-text");
    fieldsWrap.appendChild(lblFb);

    var ta = document.createElement("textarea");
    ta.id = "cvm-fb-text";
    ta.placeholder = "What's working? What's missing?";
    fieldsWrap.appendChild(ta);

    var status = document.createElement("div");
    status.className = "cvm-feedback-status";
    fieldsWrap.appendChild(status);

    var actions = document.createElement("div");
    actions.className = "cvm-fb-actions";

    var cancel = document.createElement("button");
    cancel.type = "button";
    cancel.className = "cvm-fb-cancel";
    cancel.textContent = "Close";
    cancel.addEventListener("click", togglePanel);

    var submit = document.createElement("button");
    submit.type = "button";
    submit.className = "cvm-fb-submit";
    submit.textContent = "Submit";

    actions.appendChild(cancel);
    actions.appendChild(submit);
    fieldsWrap.appendChild(actions);

    // Thank-you overlay — hidden by default, swapped in for the fields
    // group on successful submit. The page never reloads or navigates;
    // the overlay auto-hides after 3 s and the fields restore for the
    // next visit.
    var thankYouDiv = document.createElement("div");
    thankYouDiv.className = "cvm-fb-thankyou";
    thankYouDiv.style.display = "none";
    thankYouDiv.style.cssText = "padding:24px 16px;text-align:center;";
    thankYouDiv.innerHTML =
      '<div style="width:48px;height:48px;background:#E8F5E9;border-radius:50%;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;margin:0 auto 16px auto;">' +
      '<span style="font-size:24px;">✓</span></div>' +
      '<div style="font-size:16px;font-weight:600;color:#003F6B;margin-bottom:8px;">Thank you!</div>' +
      '<div style="font-size:13px;color:#5A6472;line-height:1.6;">Your feedback has been received.<br>We appreciate you taking the time.</div>';
    panel.appendChild(thankYouDiv);

    if (!CFG.enabled) {
      status.className = "cvm-feedback-status info";
      status.textContent =
        "To enable feedback recording: create a Google Form and set " +
        "CVM_FEEDBACK_FORM_ID and entry IDs in ~/.Renviron. " +
        "See setup.R for instructions.";
      submit.disabled = true;
    } else {
      submit.addEventListener("click", function() {
        var text = (ta.value || "").trim();
        if (!text) {
          status.className = "cvm-feedback-status err";
          status.textContent = "Please enter some feedback before submitting.";
          return;
        }

        var FORM_ID = CFG.form_id || "";
        var ENTRY_TAB = CFG.entry_tab || "";
        var ENTRY_COURSE = CFG.entry_course || "";
        var ENTRY_FEEDBACK = CFG.entry_feedback || "";

        if (!FORM_ID) {
          status.className = "cvm-feedback-status err";
          status.textContent = "Feedback form not configured.";
          return;
        }

        // Build prefilled formResponse URL. The hidden-iframe POST that
        // preceded this code path was being silently dropped by Google's
        // X-Frame-Options policy; opening the URL in a tiny offscreen
        // window forces the request to actually run, then we close the
        // window after a beat so the user sees nothing more than the
        // thank-you state.
        var formUrl = "https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/" + FORM_ID + "/formResponse";
        var params = new URLSearchParams();
        if (ENTRY_TAB) params.set(ENTRY_TAB, sel.value || "");
        if (ENTRY_COURSE) params.set(ENTRY_COURSE, CFG.course_code || "");
        if (ENTRY_FEEDBACK) params.set(ENTRY_FEEDBACK, text);
        params.set("submit", "Submit");

        var win = window.open(
          formUrl + "?" + params.toString(),
          "_blank",
          "width=1,height=1,left=-1000,top=-1000"
        );
        setTimeout(function() {
          try { if (win) win.close(); } catch (e) {}
        }, 2000);

        // In-panel thank-you overlay. The page never reloads or
        // navigates; we hide the form group, show the overlay, and then
        // 3 s later auto-close the panel and restore the form so the
        // widget is fresh next time the user opens it.
        submit.disabled = true;
        ta.value = "";
        sel.selectedIndex = 0;
        status.className = "cvm-feedback-status ok";
        status.textContent = "";

        var formFields = panel.querySelector(".cvm-fb-fields");
        var thankYou   = panel.querySelector(".cvm-fb-thankyou");
        if (formFields) formFields.style.display = "none";
        if (thankYou)   thankYou.style.display   = "block";

        setTimeout(function() {
          panel.classList.remove("open");
          // Reset for the next open; staggered slightly so the close
          // animation completes before the form re-appears.
          setTimeout(function() {
            if (formFields) formFields.style.display = "block";
            if (thankYou)   thankYou.style.display   = "none";
            submit.disabled = false;
            status.textContent = "";
          }, 400);
        }, 3000);
      });
    }

    document.body.appendChild(panel);
  }

  function togglePanel() {
    var panel = document.querySelector(".cvm-feedback-panel");
    if (!panel) return;
    panel.classList.toggle("open");
  }

  function init() {
    buildButton();
    buildPanel();
  }
  if (document.readyState === "loading") {
    document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", init);
  } else {
    init();
  }
})();
</script>
```
